Text:
"  ,     

Shaykh Muhammad ibn 'Abdullatf l Ash-Shaykh said:

"Allh - blessed and exalted be He - deals with His servants according to how they place Him as it relates to themselves.

Whoever reveres the command of Allh, obeys Him, avoids His prohibitions, and fears Him in secret and in public, Allh will be pleased with him and grant him happiness.

But whomsoever opposes His commands and embarks upon His prohibitions, and gives precedence to his desires over obedience to his Master, Allh will take retribution against him and distance him. As you act, so shall you be treated; an appropriate recompense. And your Lord is not at all unjust to (His) slaves."

 Durar as-Siniyyah fil Ajwibah an-Najdiah (14/289)"

Text:
" AL MARKAZ AS SALAFI LEICESTERSHIRE FUNDRAISING APPEAL

Help Establish a Salafi Markaz (Centre) & Place of Worship in Leicester! 

 Donation Details at the bottom of the Tweet.

 Our Goal

Alhamdulillah, the Salafi dawah has a long history in the City of Leicester, with lessons and gatherings being held for over two decades. Many people in the community have greatly benefited from these durs & halaqt and the opportunity to study the Quran and Authentic Sunnah upon the understanding of the Sahabah & Salaf (righteous predecessors).

By the permission of Allah, a venue has now been earmarked where a dedicated Salafi Markaz and place of worship can be established.

We are now seeking the support of the community to help us secure and prepare this space so that it can serve as a centre for prayer, learning, and dawah.

 What This Markaz Will Be Used For?

The aim is to establish a simple and beneficial space that will be used for:

 Regular Islamic lessons and Study Circles
 Propagating the Quran and Authentic Sunnah
 Community Gatherings for beneficial knowledge & Tawun upon Birr & Taqwa
 A place where Muslims can learn their Religion correctly
 Working towards Establishing the 5 daily prayers in Congregation

This Markaz will, in sh Allh, serve as a place of worship and learning for the local community.

 Why Your Support Matters?

Establishing a place where people can gather to learn their religion and remember Allah brings ongoing benefit to the community in this life & the next.

The Messenger of Allah  said:
When a person dies, his deeds come to an end except for three: ongoing charity, beneficial knowledge, or a righteous child who supplicates for him. (Sahih Muslim)

Helping establish a hub of ilm (knowledge) & ibdah (worship) can be a form of ongoing charity (Sadaqah Jriyah).

 How You Can Help

If you are able, you can support this effort by:

- Making a donation
- Sharing this fundraiser with others
- Making du that Allah allows this Markaz to be established

 HOW TO DONATE 

 GoFundMe:


 PayPal:


 Bank Transfer:
Name: Clear Guidance Ltd
Sort Code: 40-28-06
Account Number: 45387531

International Transfer
IBAN: GB91HBUK40280645387531
BIC/SWIFT: HBUKGB4B or HBUKGB4BXXX

May Allh reward everyone who supports this effort and place Barakah in it.",

" Alamdulillh, after years of work, my upcoming book OPERATION ARAB SPRING is finally near completion, inshAllh. Its one of the biggest and most important projects Ive ever worked on  A raw, unapologetic unveiling of the hidden hands, the real agendas, and the brutal truths buried beneath the bloodshed of the so-called spontaneous Arab "revolutions" that shattered the Muslim world.

This is more than just a book. It's a thoroughly documented expos. No wild theories, no fantasies  only hard truths and insidious documented plots and betrayals that many would rather keep buried.

Text:
" 

Asalamualaikum wa rahmatullahi wa barakaatuhu 

We hope and pray you are in the best of eemaan. The masjid has some great news to share, and we pray this assists in us pushing on and spreading the dawa more throughout Manchester. 

We are in the process of finalising a lease on a brand-new bookshop and dawa centre. Its not been easy to find something but we have tried to find the best place possible to accommodate both enough books, free literature as well as allowing small sittings and potentially lessons in the future by taken adjacent buildings.

We will announce the location in due course due to the sensitive nature of negotiations. However as we are at the stage of agreeing terms we thought to bring this to your attention so that you may contribute to such a blessed dawa centric activity. 

Sahl bin Sa'd (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet () said to 'Ali (May Allah be pleased with him), "By Allah, if a single person is guided by Allah through you, it will be better for you than a whole lot of red camels." Recorded in Al-Bukhari and Muslim (Riyad as-Salihin 1379).

In order to set up the bookshop we estimate that it will cost us approximately 42,000. This includes the legal fees, deposit on the building, the full refurbishment costs (electrical, plumbing, lighting, flooring, ceiling) furniture fixings, signage (external and internal) and then most important of all the books to stock and dawa material; as well as further stock to sell in order to raise funds to sustain the centre.

Please note our goal is only to make enough money so that it can carry out its main purpose, which is to call to Islam. Alongside the weekly city centre dawa stall we have, this will hopefully form a good central hub for people who are maybe new to Islam or want more information.
 
So we ask that you donate generously if you are able, to this noble cause of spreading Islam and teaching it to people - and rectifying the basics of the religion and assisting Muslims all over Manchester to return to the sunnah.

 :

Account number: 21981312
Sort code: 04-06-05

" Generosity in the Month of Raman

 Sh Abu Fuayl Abdur Raqb ( ).

 

 - Seeking nearness to Allh by way of giving in charity and being plentiful in generosity in this blessed month of Raman.
- Believe in Allh and His Messenger and spend out of that in which He has made you successors. For those who have believed among you and spent, there will be a great reward. [Srah al-Hadd 57:7]
- So fear Allh as much as you are able and listen and obey and spend [in the way of Allh]; it is better for your selves. And whoever is protected from the stinginess of his soul - it is those who will be the successful. [Srah at-Taghbun 64:16]
- Small deeds that will benefit us in the hereafter and protect us from the punishment of Allh.
- How Allh cultivates charity given from lawful earnings.
- So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it. And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it. [Srah az-Zalzalah 99:7-8]
- The meaning of .
- A adth that highlights the outcome of a deed that emanates from fear of Allh, mercy, love or sincerity, even if it is small.
- Focusing on recitation and implementation of the Qurn in Raman.
- The reason for the Messengers  increased generosity in the month of Raman.
- Fruits of reciting and reflecting upon the Qurn.
- The Messengers  detachment from the duny.",

Text:
" The bid'ah of the Murji'ah is more harmful than that of the Khawarij. 

The scholars of Ahl al-Sunnah warned strongly about the bid'ah of Irja, to the extent that many of the Salaf considered it more dangerous than the innovation of the Khawarij.

Why?

 The Khawarij reach a limit in misguidance, but the Murji'ah have no boundary; they gradually strip the religion away, piece by piece.

 The Murji'ah open the door to zandaqah (heresy) and disbelief, by emptying iman of action.

 They corrupt the understanding of the religion while appearing outwardly agreeable, even aligning with rulers and worldly interests.

 They widen the door to false interpretations, distorting the texts without restraint.

 They give false security to sinners, claiming salvation despite wrongdoing, resembling the Jews who said:

  "The Fire will not touch us except for a number of days." (al-Baqarah: 80)

Because of this, many of the Salaf (such as al-Nakha'i) said:

"Fitnat al-Murji'ah is more feared upon this Ummah than that of the Azariqah (extreme Khawarij)."

And others said:

"The Khawarij are more excusable to me than the Murji'ah."

The reality is that both groups are harmful, but:

 The Khawarij corrupt through extremism and rebellion.
 The Murjiah corrupt the very foundation of iman itself.

And corruption of the foundation is more severe.

 al-Khorasaniya - Shaykh 'Abd al-Aziz al-Turayfi"

Text:
" This is Why anaf Scholars Took So Much Caution with Takfr (Obv other Madhabs too)

1 Mull Al al-Qr al-anaf   (d. 1014 AH) said:

The scholars have stated regarding the matter of declaring someone a disbeliever (takfr): if there are 99 possible interpretations indicating kufr (disbelief) and only one that is not kufr, it is better for the muft and the judge to adopt the interpretation that is not kufr.

He further explained that this principle is derived from the saying of the Prophet :

Avert punishments from the Muslims as much as you can. If you find a way out for a Muslim, then let him go.

 [Shar al-Shif al-Q Iy, 2/502]

2 Imm Ibn bidn al-anaf   (d. 1252 AH) said:

The act of disbelief is a grave matter, so I do not declare a believer as a disbeliever. Whenever I find a narration stating [kufr], he is not to be declared as a kfir.

In summary: If there are aspects in an issue that necessitate declaring takfr [excommunication] and one aspect prevents him [from committing kufr], then the muft should lean towards the aspect that prevents declaring takfr on the Muslim, in order to maintain a good opinion towards the Muslim.

Additionally, in al-Bazzyyah, it is added: Except if one explicitly states an intention affirming kufr [disbelief], interpretation [which protected him from takfr] does not benefit them.

 [Radd al-Mutr al al-Durr al-Mukhtr, 4/408]"

My dear brothers and sisters...There has been much discussion regarding this matter (i.e. Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid). Some have asked: Does one prayer suffice for the other, or is it obligatory for a Muslim to perform both the Eid and Jumuah prayers?

Many who have spoken on this issue have done so without the requisite qualification to understand the evidences transmitted from the Messenger of Allh . This is because they approach the matter based on a flawed methodology: they first adopt a belief, then seek out supporting evidence. This is a mistake! The correct methodology is to derive beliefs from evidence, not to impose ones beliefs or schools of thought upon the evidence.

Therefore, I shall briefly present the adths and reports attributed to the Messenger of Allh  and the noble Companionsmay Allh be pleased with themthen explain the positions of the recognised schools of Fiqh on this issue, relying upon Allh Almighty.

So I say, with Allhs guidance, that there are both prophetic traditions (marf) and reports from the companion (mawqf) on this matter.

The first is the adth of Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allh be pleased with him), in which Muwiyah ibn Ab Sufyn (may Allh be pleased with him) asked him: Did you witness the Messenger of Allh  having two Eids occur on the same day? He replied, Yes. He asked, What did he do? Zayd said, He performed the Eid prayer, then granted a concession regarding the Jumuah prayer, saying: Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray. This adth was narrated by Amad, Ab Dwd, An-Nas, Ibn Mjah, Ad-Drim, and Al-Hkim, and it is authentic.

The second adth is from Ab Hurayrah (may Allh be pleased with him), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we will indeed perform the Jumuah. This adth was also narrated by Ab Dwd and Ibn Mjah, and it is authentic.

The third adth is from Ibn Umar (may Allh be pleased with him), who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh . He led the people in prayer, then said: Whoever wishes to attend the Jumuah may do so, and whoever wishes to refrain may do so. This adth was narrated by Ibn Mjah. A-abarn also transmitted it with the wording: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh  on the day of Eid Al-Fir and Friday. The Messenger of Allh  led them in the Eid prayer, then turned to face them and said: O people, you have achieved good and reward. Indeed, we are holding the Jumuah. Whoever wishes to join us may do so, and whoever wishes to return to his family may do so. This narration is also authentic.

The fourth adth is from Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them both), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we are gathering, in-sh-Allh. This adth is also authentic and narrated by Ibn Mjah.

From the reports of the Companions: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the day. Then we proceeded to attend Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we mentioned this to him, he said: He acted in accordance with the Sunnah. This was reported by Ab Dwd, and by Ibn Khuzaymah with a different wording and with an addition: Ibn Az-Zubayr said: I saw Umar ibn Al-Khab, when two Eids coincided, do the same."
"
--------------

Text:
""Tonight is the night most hoped to be Laylat al-Qadr.
Do not waste this night. Do not indulge in arm.
Stand before Allah in your alh and prolong your standing before Him. Increase in dhikr, alh, du, and the recitation of the Qurn.
Make du and pour your heart out to Allah. Do not hesitate for even a second.
May Allah grant us and you success."
"
--------------

Ramadhan 1447 (Day 27) 
The Night Most Hoped to Be Laylat Al-Qadr 

This is the view held by the majority of scholars, and it is considered the strongest and most well-known opinion. The evidence includes the following hadiths:

1 The Prophet  said:
((      ))
If someone is searching for it, let him look for it on the twenty-seventh night. [Sahih al-Jami, no. 2920] 

2 Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) reported that a man came to the Prophet  and said: O Messenger of Allah, I am an old and weak man, and standing in prayer at night is difficult for me. Tell me a night in which Allah may allow me to reach Laylat Al-Qadr. The Prophet  said:
(( ))
Focus on the seventh night (meaning the 27th). [Authenticated by Ibn Rajab in Lataif Al-Maarif and by Al-Arnaut in his checking of Musnad Ahmad] 

3 Muawiyah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah  said:
((    ))
Laylat Al-Qadr is the twenty-seventh night. [Sahih Abi Dawud, no. 1386] 

4 Ubayy ibn Kab (may Allah be pleased with him) was certainhe even sworethat it was the 27th night. When he was asked how he knew this, he said: By the sign the Messenger of Allah  told us: the sun rises the next morning without strong rays. [Reported by Muslim, no. 762] 

5 The Prophet  led the companions in night prayer during some nights until one-third of the night had passed, and on some nights until half the night. But on the 27th night, he led them in prayer until morning. [Authenticated by al-Albani in As-Silsilah 7/101] 

6 On the 27th night, the Prophet  gathered his family and wives (unlike the 23rd and 25th nights) and prayed with them until they feared they might miss the pre-dawn meal (suhoor).
[Sahih Ibn Majah, no. 1100] 

 The 27th night begins at sunset of the 26th day of Ramadan.

We ask Allah to allow us and all Muslims to reach it and to be among those who stand in prayer during it with faith and sincere hope for reward.

Source:

Text:
""Wahhabism is the tool of extremism and modern day terrorism. 

It began as theology fused with power.

Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab was an 18th-century cleric from Najd who preached uncompromising monotheism and condemned what he saw as religious deviation. But his defining weapon was not spirituality. It was takfir: declaring other Muslims outside the fold.

And even inside his own home, there was alarm.

His father, Abd al-Wahhab ibn Sulayman, reportedly warned people not to follow his sons extremism. His own brother, Sulayman ibn Abd al-Wahhab, wrote an entire refutation:

      
Al-Sawaiq al-Ilahiyya fi al-Radd ala al-Wahhabiyya
(Divine Thunderbolts in Refutation of the Wahhabis)

In it, he warned that reckless takfir leads to bloodshed. He compared the movement to early Kharijite extremism  religion weaponized against Muslims. And, that is exactly what happened in the following years.

Then came 1744.

The pact between Ibn Abd al-Wahhab and Muhammad bin Saud in Diriyah.

The formula was simple and ruthless:

Al-Sheikh holds religion.
Al-Saud holds power.

Through that alliance, theology became state machinery.

Expansion was framed as purification. Campaigns like the attack on Karbala in 1802 are documented in multiple chronicles. Sectarian violence was justified as religious correction. Unity meant obedience. Dissent meant deviance.

That fusion : Religion as political legitimacy became the backbone of the Saudi state model.

Fast forward to the 20th century.

Oil wealth globalized the doctrine. Religious institutions expanded. Clerical authority was centralized. Universities such as Imam Muhammad ibn Saud Islamic University became exporters of a rigid interpretation of Salafism.

Then came Afghanistan.

During the Soviet war, Saudi religious rhetoric aligned with U.S. anti-communist strategy. Arab Afghans were mobilized under religious banners. The ideological infrastructure created centuries earlier proved adaptable to modern geopolitics.

From that environment emerged figures like Osama bin Laden, founder of Al-Qaeda. Later movements from Al-Qaeda to ISIS drew heavily from Takfiri Wahhabi logic: excommunication as a political weapon to justify jihad and murder. 

This does not mean Ibn Abd al-Wahhab sat in the 18th century planning 9/11.

It means that when you normalize declaring Muslims apostates, and when state power protects that logic, you build an intellectual ecosystem extremists can later radicalize further.

The 9/11 hijackers, European terror cells, sectarian militias in Iraq and Syria  they justified violence using theological frameworks built on takfir and militant purification.

That ideological pipeline matters.

Throughout modern Middle Eastern politics, religion was repeatedly used as leverage:

Against Arab nationalism.
Against Jamal Abdulnasser.
During Cold War alignments.
During regional rivalries.

Each time, theology served strategy.

And now?

When Saudi enters political conflict whether in Yemen, Qatar, or against the UAE  the instinctive tool is familiar: religious framing. Accuse. Label. Delegitimize.

Same method. New arena.

History shows the pattern:

When religion becomes state armor, power hides behind piety.
When dissent becomes heresy, control becomes sacred.
And when takfir becomes political currency, violence follows.

The earliest warning came from inside the family itself. But were silenced so Bin Saud and Alsheikh can carry out their gruesome acts of genocide against the Shia people in Alhassa and then in 1802, Saudi-Wahhabi forces attacked Karbala and killed under the justification of Takfir."
"
--------------

Zio-Emiratism is a tool of 

The reality, as i stated before, is that many of these  funded Emiratis hate Islam and Tawheed

May Allah have mercy on Shaykh Muhammad Ibn Abdulwahhab

He is free from the lies you associate with him,

Text:
"#FreeAafiaNow #BringAafiaHome
The US war on Muslim women.
The Story of Aafia Siddiqui . A Mother, A Sister, A Prisoner. The most oppressed woman in the world.

She was kidnapped. Sold. Raped. Her children were torn from her arms and sold alongside her, and what followed is one of the most harrowing stories of injustice in modern history.
Aafia Siddiqui, a Pakistani neuroscientist and mother of three, was abducted along with her young children. The Pakistani government sold her and her babies to the CIA for $55,000, a transaction that treated a mother and her children as commodities.

What was done to those children defies imagination.

Her infant son, Sulaiman, was just six months old when a CIA agent killed him, slamming his head to the ground in front of his six-year-old brother, Ahmed. Ahmed was then taken to Afghanistan, tortured, and imprisoned for five years.

Her daughter, Maryam, was only three years old. She was forcibly placed with a Christian family who changed her name and kept her for seven years.

As for Aafia herself, she was tortured, raped, and is currently serving an 86-year sentence in a US federal prison. Never once charged with terrorism. Never convicted of a violent crime.

This is United States terrorism on Muslim women. A mother, a daughter, a sister, targeted, brutalised, and discarded. Her only crime was her faith and her identity.

And what is perhaps most shocking is the silence.

The silence of human rights organizations. The silence of NGOs. The silence of the international community, the very institutions that exist to speak for the voiceless, that march and campaign and raise millions in the name of justice. Where are they for Aafia? Where are they for her children?

Their silence is not neutrality. Their silence is complicity.

This is what was done to our sister. May she never be forgotten."

Text:
"#ISIS #AlQaeda #Iran
After focusing on Syria in its 21 Feb leadership message, urging rival Islamists to join IS due to the Sharaa government's alleged subservience to US, IS has shifted its attention to Al-Qaeda. 

In the latest issue of its weekly paper Al-Naba on 26 Feb, IS claims Al-Qaeda is subservient to Iran, encouraging Al-Qaeda followers/sympathisers to wake up, and presumably switch to IS instead.

IS points to Al-Qaedas 4 Feb message, which called for attacks against US military bases in the Middle East, as evidence of this alleged loyalty to Iran. 
Many anti-Al-Qaeda  jihadist as well as analysts at the time interpreted this call as a sign of Al-Qaedas indirect alignment with Iran, particularly since Al-Qaeda leadership is believed to be based there.

By trying to discredit both Al-Qaeda and the Sharaa government in Syria - the rival global jihadist project and the pragmatic Islamist project, respectively - IS aims to position itself as the only true Muslim group that remains committed to Sharia and that has not allegedly compromised for political gains. 

IS wants to paint itself as the righteous choice for those seeking a genuine Muslim cause"

Text:
"'    

The Ashari view of Gods actions is rooted in a strict form of divine command theory (DCT), where no logically possible act is deemed unbefitting of Him. This approach raises deeper questions about the nature of truth and divine guidance. 

For example, on the Ash'ari view, Does God have to tell the truth? This question gets to the core of a deep problem that underlies their strict DCT view. 

Ill break it down in simple terms here. But first, a disclaimer:

                .               .

 B:

- Asharis believe that no logically possible act is unbefitting of God. Any action you can possibly conceive of is an equally possible and valid expression of His will as any other. On this view, there is nothing about God that makes the creation of this world any more likely than a world with nothing but a pile of dog feces created for absolutely no reason (excuse the stark example, but it helps illustrate the point clearly). He chose to create this world instead of the feces world for literally no reason at all.

- For example, on this view, a world that contains nothing but an innocent baby suffering in Hell for an eternity for absolutely no reason is perfectly befitting of God and His perfection. It is axiologically identical to a world where the righteous are rewarded and only the sinful are punished. Both possible realities are equally befitting of God, and theres absolutely no reason or wisdom that can explain why He chose to do one instead of the other.

- Even in terms of our deeds: God doesnt prefer one deed over another, unless by prefer you mean He just arbitrarily willed for it to happen (for no reason). The most pious worshipper to have ever lived is identical to Iblis in terms of Gods preference or love. Unless we understand love as Gods will to reward the worshipper, which again, is completely arbitrary and could have gone in the exact opposite direction with the worshiper getting punished and Iblis getting rewarded. Both equally possible and equally befitting of God. The only reason we assume that one is better than the other is because thats what weve become accustomed to.

So, on the Ashari view, Gods actions are completely arbitrary. He doesnt act for any specific reason. He creates clouds through which He creates rain, but He didnt create the clouds for that reason. It just happens to be the case that he created clouds followed by rain.

  

So, where does the truthfulness question come in?

Well, if all logically possible acts are equally befitting of God, and theres nothing about Him that explains why He chooses to act one way instead of another except for brute arbitrary will, then how can you be sure He tells the truth?

They cant appeal to perfection, as Al-Juwayni and others explain, because then the entire foundation collapses and we could (as we do) similarly appeal to perfection when explaining why any of the above possibilities is unbefitting of God.

The typical Ashari move here is to argue that its logically impossible for God to lie. They say that Gods created speech (like the Arabic Qur'an) necessarily indicates His internal speech.

Therefore, lying is impossible because created speech cannot conflict with the internal meaning it indicates.

But even if thats true, it doesnt solve the real issue, namely: How do you know  that this SPECIFIC instance of "created" speechlike the Qur'ancorresponds to Gods internal speech?

  " "

In response, Asharis would typically appeal to miracles. 

The idea is that when you see a prophet perform miracles, God creates an unshakable certainty in your mind that the prophet is truthful.

The logic is that miracles are like a seal of approval from God proving that the prophet speaks the truth.

They use the common analogy of the king to explain this:

Imagine a king in a grand gathering. A man stands up and says, Im the kings messenger and will speak on behalf of him. To prove it, the man turns to the king and says, O King, if Im truthful, stand up and sit back down as a sign. When the king, who doesnt usually do such a thing, actually complies, everyone in the room instantly knows the man is truthful.

Asharis argue that this analogous to what happens when God displays miracles through a prophet. It creates undeniable certainty that the prophet is truthful.

And of course, this is true in general, but not on Ashari grounds!

Why? Because they also believe that God acts for no reasons whatsoever and that any logically conceivable act is in line with His perfection.

This analogy only works if the king acts with purpose; if he has "reasons" behind his actions. If the kings actions are just randomif he stands up and sits down for no reason whatsoeverthen the act doesnt prove anything.

He couldn't have done it with the intention of demonstrating the mans truthfulness because he never does one thing with the intention of achieving another in the first place.

So, God has the ability to create miracles at the hands if a false prophet, and on the Ashari view, whether He would choose to do so or not is entirely arbitrary: no one act is more preferable or likely than the other with respect to His nature and perfection.

And as Ibn Taymiyya says, appealing to "necessity" to justify knowing one thing over another, when theres no objective difference between the two whatsoever, ultimately destroys any reliable basis for knowledge.

 " " 

Finally, one of the most common responses they present is that if false prophets were allowed to perform miracles, it would undermine Gods ability to reveal truth to humanity.

They reason that if false prophets could perform miracles, it would blur the line between truth and falsehood, making it impossible for God to guide people to the truth through miracles.

So they argue that this would compromise Gods omnipotence, as it would entail that He is unable to demonstrate the truthfulness of a prophet, and therefore, it could never happen.

However, this argument falls apart when examined against their broader theological framework. Heres why:

1.       

The first issue is that the Ashari view itself makes the very act of demonstrating a prophets truthfulness through miracles impossible. Their framework denies that God acts with purpose or intention, which means there is no reason behind any action He performs. But for miracles to serve as evidence of prophethood, there must be a purposeful link between the miracle and the claim it validates.

For example, if a king sends a messenger with a unique seal to prove his authority, the seal serves as evidence only because the king intentionally assigned it that role. Without intention, the seal is meaningless.

Similarly, miracles cannot inherently signify truthfulness unless God intentionally creates them for that purpose.

Since the Ashari view denies that God acts with reasons, it follows that demonstrating truthfulness through miracles is inherently impossible in their framework. And if an act is inherently impossible, divine power does not "attach" to itjust as it does not "attach" to creating a square circle.

This makes their argument self-destruct. If their framework already excludes the possibility of demonstrating truth through miracles, then allowing miracles for false prophets would not compromise Gods power, as it cannot attach to something impossible in the first place.

2.    () 

When confronted with the problem of intentionality, some Asharis respond by appealing to talazumthe idea that one thing can logically necessitate another. 

They argue that miracles could inherently serve as evidence of truthfulness through a built-in necessary connection, without requiring any purposeful act from God. For example, they might say that just as knowledge necessitates life, a miracle could necessitate the truthfulness of a prophets claim.

But this argument fails because miracles do not intrinsically signify truthfulness. Their role as evidence of genuine prophethood relies on being intentionally assigned that role by God. Without such purpose or reason, a miracle could just as easily be a random act of power, with no connection to the truth of a prophets claim.

Think of it like a flag on a battlefield. A flag might signify victory, but only if it is intentionally raised for that purpose. If the flag is raised randomly or blown into the air by the wind, it no longer serves as a meaningful indicator of victory.

Similarly, for miracles to signify the truthfulness of a prophet, there must be a purposeful act linking the miracle to its role as evidence.

Without any such reasons behind God's actions, there is no necessary connection between miracles and truthfulness. Their appeal to talazum collapses because the connection between a miracle and its meaning is not intrinsic; it must be purposefully assigned.

3.       

This "compromised power" response also leads to a deeper dilemma: is it possible for God to allow a false prophet to perform miracles, or not?

They are left with two options, both of which are problematic:

)            :

This position is correct, because it aligns with the idea that Gods wisdom and perfection would never permit the validation of falsehood. However, it also contradicts their framework, which denies that any act can be inherently unbefitting or inappropriate for God.

For them, all logically possible acts are equally befitting of God's perfection, so they cannot consistently claim that God would never allow miracles at the hands of a false prophet without such an act being impossible in itself.

)           :

This position compromises Gods omnipotence by making something possible in itself (creating miracles at the hands of a false prophet) impossible for God. It implies a deficiency in divine power.
----------------------------------

In short, the Ashari stance on the arbitrariness of Gods actions, along with their belief that no possible act is "unbefitting" of Him, leads to serious foundational issues with the concepts of truth and divine guidance."

For them, all logically possible acts are equally befitting of God's perfection, so they cannot consistently claim that God would never allow miracles at the hands of a false prophet without such an act being impossible in itself. ) : This position compromises Gods omnipotence by making something possible in itself (creating miracles at the hands of a false prophet) impossible for God. It implies a deficiency in divine power. ---------------------------------- In short, the Ashari stance on the arbitrariness of Gods actions, along with their belief that no possible act is "unbefitting" of Him, leads to serious foundational issues with the concepts of truth and divine guidance."

Text:
"**Shaykh Abd al-Rahman al-Barrak on UK Student Loans**

The Shaykh was asked about student loans in the UK with these features:

 Issued by the government to support students  
 Interest is applied from the moment the loan is taken  
 Repayment starts after graduation and employment, if income reaches a threshold  
 If income is low, repayment is not required  
 The loan may be written off after 30 years, or due to death or inability to work  

Verdict from the Shaykh:

It is not permissible to take this loan.

Reason:

 It involves interest paid by the borrower  
 It is a complicated contract with many conditions and issues  

Even with income-based repayment and possible write-off, the presence of interest means it should be avoided entirely."

Text:
"A Note Regarding my Trip to the USA 8th - 13th April, 2026

It has once again been a truly enjoyable visit to the USA. I extend my sincere thanks to our beloved communities in Roanoke, Virginia, and Columbus, Ohio, for organizing and facilitating the events over the past few days. I am deeply grateful for their outstanding hospitality.

By the blessing of Allh, I had the opportunity to meet communities being cultivated upon the way of the Scholarshonouring them, loving them, and recognizing their rightful status. May Allh continue to grant them success.

The dawah in the USA is clearly growing. I ask Allh to bless the communities of Roanoke and Columbus, and all Salaf communities, as well as all our respected and noble brothersthe honourable dut and teachers in the USA who strive tirelessly to benefit the Muslims across the country. They teach the Quran and Sunnah, connect the people to the Scholars, and nurture within them love and respect for them: Ab Muhammad al-Maghrab, Abul Hasan Mlik, Hasan Somali, Abul-Abbs Ms Richardson, Anwar Wright, Umar Quinn, Rasheed Barbee, Raha Batts, Jaml Finch, Hamza AbdurRazzq, Mustaf George, Ab Ansa Abdul Hakeem, and others among the teachers upon the same commendable path whom I may have missed.

For decades, our dawah in the West has been firmly built upon returning to the Scholarsmaintaining strong ties with them, seeking their guidance in our affairs, and recognizing our position beneath them. By the permission of Allh, if we remain steadfast upon this path, we will continue to grow and progress, our dawah will only become stronger, and we will find the sweetness of brotherhood, love and unity between us.

As for those who are no longer able to uphold this way, and have instead opened doors to corruption and misplaced boldness against the scholars that we long worked within the dawah to keep firmly shut for yearsand in fact rebuked opposers who often attempt to open themthen do not concern yourselves with such individuals or their supporters issuing lengthy and meandering justifications for their weak and erroneous positions lacking resolveat a time when nothing but firm resolve itself is the requirement.

Ab Muadh Taqweem

25th Shawwl 1447h
13th April, 2026

  "

Text:
"A Reminder of Fairness and Justice in Defending the People of Knowledge
 
Among the first to be targeted with attacks and assaults on his status by Amjad Rafq was the noble Shaykh, the Faqh Araft al-Muammad (may Allh preserve him).
 
By the praise of Allh, his advice and refutations bore fruit, and today you can see the reality of what he had warned against, in what Amjad Rafq is now displaying of transgressions towards the status of knowledge and its people.
 
I wish to draw attention to a matter that may go unnoticed in the writings of some of the brothers and callers in the West:
 
Despite what we see, and it is commendable, of their responses to Amjad Rafqs attacks on Shaykh al-Allmah Abdullh al-Bukhr (may Allh preserve him), we hardly see a similar defence of their brother, Shaykh Araft al-Muammad (may Allh preserve him).
 
Even though Amjad Rafq had previously attacked him, accused him of lying, attributed to him weakness in knowledge, and described him with offensive terms, many of them did not move to refute this falsehood or stop this transgression.
 
It would have been more fitting, in upholding justice and fairness, that they also defend the honour of Shaykh Araft (may Allh preserve him), as he was among the first to confront the errors of Amjad Rafq and warn against his methodology.

Written by: Jafar al-Hshim al-Awz
@Arafatbinhassan @ZubayrAbbasi @yasin_ibn_jamal @AbooSuhailah"

Text:
"A Warning To Those That Defend Themselves But Are Silent When The Truth & It's People Are Violated 

Ibn Qayyim    said: 

"And what religion, and what good, is there in a person who sees that which Allah has prohibited being violated, His boundaries being ignored and transgressed upon, His religion being abandoned, and the Sunnah of His Messenger being rejected? Yet his heart is unmoved, and his tongue remains silent. Indeed, he is a mute devil, just like one who speaks with falsehood is an articulate devil. 

Has the religion (of Allah) ever been tested with anything other than the likes of these people? Those who, so long as their food and (positions of) leadership are secured and protected, have no concern for what happens to the religion of Allah. The best among them may feel slight sadness, but should he be challenged regarding something he holds dearwhether concerning his status or his wealthhe will strive and toil to the utmost of his ability, using the three levels of rejection and rebuke to defend himself, (i.e. they use their hands, their tongues, and their heart to; change, repel, reject, and rebuke that evil). 

These individuals, though they have fallen in the sight of Allah and are under His anger, have been tested in this worldly life with the greatest of trials, yet they do not feel it. Certainly, for sure, it is the death of the hearts. For indeed, the more alive the heart is, the stronger its anger is for the sake of Allah and His Messenger, and the more complete its defence of the religion shall be."

['Ilm al-Muwaqq'n 'an Rabb al-'lamn Ibn al-Qayyim 2/177] translated by Abu Ukkashah Abdulhakeem"

Text:
"A questioner asks: What is the ruling on offering congratulations and gifts on the occasion of Valentines Day, which falls in mid-February each year?

All praise is due to Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah and upon his family and companions. As for what follows:

This holiday is a festival of the kuffar and Mushrikeen. Even if it is called Valentines Day, it is a festival claimed by the Greeks, an ancient festival for a deity they worshipped besides Allah. 

Whether this holiday was for a deity they worshipped or merely for what they call and claim to be love, then congratulating others on it, celebrating this day, and honoring it is forbidden and not permissible.

We, the people of Islam, have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha. It is not permissible to congratulate others for any Eid besides these two. So congratulating others on this day and honoring it is not allowed; it is forbidden. You are not permitted to congratulate anyone on it, nor are you permitted to accept congratulations from anyone on this day. Rather, it is obligatory upon you and every Muslim to reject anyone who congratulates you on this day and to clarify to people that congratulating others on this day is forbidden and not permissible, and that it is a form of imitating the Kuffar and Mushrikeen in their false festivals, which are based on falsehood, disbelief, immorality, and wickedness.

How can they claim it is a day of love and magnify love while they are among the most hostile people toward Islam and Muslims, waging war against the religion of Allah and against the people of Islam? A Muslim should not be deceived by their labels or slogans. 

These Kuffar and wicked people are among the furthest from what is called love. They are the ones killing us, killing our brothers in Syria, Iraq, and everywhere, yet they claim this day is Valentines Day.

Therefore, it is not permissible to congratulate others on this day, nor is it permissible for a Muslim to honor it, give gifts on it, or accept gifts on it. It is a forbidden day; honoring it, celebrating it, and offering congratulations for such false festivals is forbidden.

We have only two Eids: Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha, and these are the ones in which it is permissible to rejoice.

Likewise, commercial shops that sell gifts such as flowers, teddy bears, and similar items on this day, some people even change their pages or displays to red or use symbols associated with celebrating this day, selling flowers or anything that assists in honoring this day is not permissible. It is forbidden and is considered helping in falsehood. 

Allah says: Cooperate in righteousness and piety, and do not cooperate in sin and transgression.

Whoever sells such flowers with the intention of honoring that day, his sale is forbidden, and the money he earns from it is unlawful. Every body nourished by unlawful earnings, the Fire is more deserving of it. So it is not permissible to take such money, nor to sell flowers, change displays, alter slogans, or use symbols such as roses or the color red for this day, for these are forbidden and reprehensible matters.

It is obligatory upon a Muslim to honor the symbols of Allah, to honor the religion of Allah, to show disavowal of these Kuffar and wicked people, and to distinguish himself from them in their festivals and their false, misguided beliefs. For resemblance in outward matters leads to resemblance in inward matters.

We ask Allah for well-being and safety. 

Shaykh Khalid al Fulaij (may Allah free him)"

Text:
"A voice called out:

This is how the funerals of the Imms of Ahl al-Sunnah should be!

The crowd burst into tears at these words. People threw their handkerchiefs and turbans onto the wagon carrying the Shaykhs body. Over 200,000 men where estimated to be in attendance. 

Salt al-Ghib (funeral prayer in absentia) was preformed for him in most regions of the Muslim world, near and far. Some travellers reported that the news of his death had even reached the remotest areas of China so much so a public call was made for the funeral prayer in these places: 

The prayer for the interpreter of the Qurn

- Abridged from Ibn Rajabs account of Ibn Taymiyyahs death, from his work: Dhayl Dabaqt al-Hanbilah.",

Text:
"Al-Fudayl ibn Iyad, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
If you see oppressors taking revenge on other oppressors, stand and observe in astonishment.

When you see the greatest enemies of Islam in this era, those with the most blood of Muslims on their hands, turning against one another and weakening themselves through their conflicts, let your heart be soothed and turn to Allah in gratitude.

Sometimes, Allah soothes hearts by letting oppressors and tyrants orchestrate their own downfall through their conflicts and wars.

We make the wrongdoers destructive to one another because of their misdeeds. (6:129)

The parties involved in this war, the US along with their awliy, the leaders of the Gulf, as well as the Rafidah and the Yahud, are enemies, equally guilty and steeped in crimes against Islam and Muslims. Their hands are stained with the blood of countless innocents, and to true believers who grasp the depth of their kufr and the enormity of their crimes, each is as condemned as the next, none surpassing the other in guilt. They vied ruthlessly in their oppression and in betraying the Muslims.

Malik ibn Dinar, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
"I read in the revealed Books of Allah that Allah, the Exalted, said: I destroy My enemies through My enemies, and then I destroy them through My awliy."

May Allah prolong their strife so that they are preoccupied with one another and weakened, and may He raise to leadership on this earth the Muslims they betrayed, oppressed, and harmed, so that Tawheed may be established across the earth!

May Allah safeguard every innocent Muslim wherever this conflict occurs!

Ahmad Musa Jibril
2 Shawwal 1447 AH"

Text:
"Allmah Ibn Taymiyyah was asked about saying L Ilha ill Allh 70,000 times and gifting it to the dead. He responded: If he does it like this 70,000 times, or more, or less, and gifts it Allh will benefit them with it. And this has no [basis] in neither authentic nor weak Hadth.

 [Majm al-Fatw, 24/323, translation from @ahnaafservices Twitter]

Benefits:

1. Ihda As-Sawab (Urdu Isaal e Sawab), or gifting deeds to the dead, is something that was generally considered permissible.

2. It's not necessary that every particular deed related to Sadaqah Jariyah is proven from the Sahabah. For ex. It's not necessary to show that the Sahabah donated an X-ray machine to a hospital with the intention of Sadaqah Jariyah.

3. A precedent in the actions of the Sahabah. - Summary- Unable to financially contribute to the Tabuk expedition, Ulbah bin Zayd (Radi Allahu Anhu) wept in prayer and sincerely offered his personal honor as charity by forgiving any Muslim who had wronged him. The next morning, the Prophet (Salallahu Alaihi Wa Sallam) informed him that Allah had recognized his pure intention and officially accepted his act of forgiveness as a highly rewarded charity. (Seerat Ibn Hisham (Vol. 2, p. 516) and Zaad al-Ma'ad by Ibn al-Qayyim (Vol. 3, p. 461), authentic.)

4. This also means, reciting the Quran and gifting it to the dead is permissible. Ibn Taymiyyah held that opinion. In our contemporary Ulama, Shaykh Muhammad bin Saleh Al-Uthaymeen said that it was permissible to gift deeds to the dead."

 Our stance is not to support either side. Rather, their internal conflict weakens their grip on the region while keeping them preoccupied with themselves. This may give our brothers and sisters in Gaza and elsewhere some breathing space and relief.

Had these countries been ruled by sincere Muslims, united in Tawd and loyalty to Allah, the Zionist entity Israel would have collapsed long ago. Instead, they remain idle while Gaza bleeds proving their betrayal beyond any doubt.

Text:
"Ben Mermarli, lawyer of Marwan Barghouti, writes:

On April 12, 2026, I visited Marwan Barghouti in my capacity as his lawyer.

What I confirmed during this visit is deeply alarming. In recent weeks, Marwan Barghouti has been subjected to three violent assaults. On April 8, in Ganot Prison, he was severely beaten and left bleeding for more than two hours. He requested medical care, but his request was denied. On March 25, he was assaulted while being transferred from Megiddo to Ganot. And on March 24, in Megiddo Prison, guards entered his cell accompanied by a dog, forced him to lie on the ground, and the dog attacked him repeatedly.

These are not isolated incidents, but rather a clear pattern of escalating violations: violence, medical neglect, and treatment that places him in serious danger.

He had a lot to say. Above all, he wanted to know more about his family and the Palestinian people, and about what is happening on both the Palestinian and Israeli sides. I tried to update him on everything I knew.

But even that conversation took place under absurd conditions: the phones were not working, so we had to shout through the glass just to hear each other.

I sat there for five hours without food or water, trying to make sure that this visit was meaningful.

This is what a legal visit looks like today: deprivation of basic rights, obstruction of communication, and disregard for even the most basic human and professional standards.

And yet, despite all of this, his mind remained sharp, focused, and fully engaged with everything happening beyond the prison walls."

Interest System Stopped in All Commercial Banks

Dr. Lutfullah Khairkhwa, Director of Banking and Financial Services at Da Afghanistan Bank, announced that the practice of usury has been eliminated across all commercial banks operating in the country.

According to Khairkhwa, the national banking system is currently undergoing a complete transition away from interest-based transactions toward a fully Islamic banking model.

Text:
"Do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. 

Shaykh Khalid al-Fulayj (may Allah free him) said in his explanation of as Sunah of al Barbahari:

Islam cannot be firmly established except upon complete submission to the texts of the Book and the Sunnah. So do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. 

Knowledge is not based on the minds and views of men; rather, it is with Allah and with His Messenger. So do not follow anything based on your desires, lest you deviate from the religion and exit Islam, then you would have no proof.

The Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) has clarified the Sunnah for his Ummah and made it clear to his companions. They are the Jamaat, and they are the great majority, and the great majority is the truth and its people. So whoever opposes the companions of Muhammad (salallahu alayhi wa salam) in anything of the Deen has disbelieved.

From this issue, an important principle is established: the texts of the Book and the Sunnah are not to be opposed by opinions, desires, tastes, spiritual feelings, or intellects. Rather, the stance of a Muslim regarding revelation is submission, belief, listening, obedience, following, and compliance, without disputing.

Then some ignorant people may come and narrate a hadith in Sahih al-Bukhari from Abu Hurairah in which the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) said:

If a fly falls into the vessel of one of you, let him immerse it, for on one of its wings is disease and on the other is cure.

Then the pretentious, the arrogant, the pseudo-intellectual, and the ignorant say: This contradicts reason. This is something impossible, how can an insect have disease in one wing and cure in the other?

We say: We believe in Allah, we believe in His Messenger (salallahu alayhi wa salam), we affirm, we hear, and we obey. Our Messenger does not speak from his own desire, rather, it is nothing but revelation revealed. So just as the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) informed us, we say: we hear and we obey.

As for you, by opposing the report of the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam):

If you know the report is authentic, then you have denied the Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) and committed disbelief by rejecting it.

And if you are ignorant, we teach you and establish the proof against you. If you persist upon your falsehood and misguidance, then you disbelieve in Allah the Most Great, because you have described the Messenger as a liar and claimed that his statement is not true.

But the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) does not speak from his own desire, rather, he speaks only revelation that is revealed.

So this is what is obligatory upon the Muslim: belief, listening, and obedience."
Reasons_אריאל כהן: ד. תמיכה בתכפיר קולקטיבי
normalized_text: "Do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. Shaykh Khalid al-Fulayj (may Allah free him) said in his explanation of as Sunah of al Barbahari: Islam cannot be firmly established except upon complete submission to the texts of the Book and the Sunnah. So do not oppose the texts with intellects or opinions. Knowledge is not based on the minds and views of men; rather, it is with Allah and with His Messenger. So do not follow anything based on your desires, lest you deviate from the religion and exit Islam, then you would have no proof. The Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) has clarified the Sunnah for his Ummah and made it clear to his companions. They are the Jamaat, and they are the great majority, and the great majority is the truth and its people. So whoever opposes the companions of Muhammad (salallahu alayhi wa salam) in anything of the Deen has disbelieved. From this issue, an important principle is established: the texts of the Book and the Sunnah are not to be opposed by opinions, desires, tastes, spiritual feelings, or intellects. Rather, the stance of a Muslim regarding revelation is submission, belief, listening, obedience, following, and compliance, without disputing. Then some ignorant people may come and narrate a hadith in Sahih al-Bukhari from Abu Hurairah in which the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) said: If a fly falls into the vessel of one of you, let him immerse it, for on one of its wings is disease and on the other is cure. Then the pretentious, the arrogant, the pseudo-intellectual, and the ignorant say: This contradicts reason. This is something impossible, how can an insect have disease in one wing and cure in the other? We say: We believe in Allah, we believe in His Messenger (salallahu alayhi wa salam), we affirm, we hear, and we obey. Our Messenger does not speak from his own desire, rather, it is nothing but revelation revealed. So just as the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) informed us, we say: we hear and we obey. As for you, by opposing the report of the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam): If you know the report is authentic, then you have denied the Messenger of Allah (salallahu alayhi wa salam) and committed disbelief by rejecting it. And if you are ignorant, we teach you and establish the proof against you. If you persist upon your falsehood and misguidance, then you disbelieve in Allah the Most Great, because you have described the Messenger as a liar and claimed that his statement is not true. But the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) does not speak from his own desire, rather, he speaks only revelation that is revealed. So this is what is obligatory upon the Muslim: belief, listening, and obedience."

Text:
"Every Muslim who is being slandered by the hypocrites/disbelievers should be defended without a doubt, or offered a word of support at a minimum (even if they dont need it). This is the bare minimum rights that any of the Mashayikh, Mujahideen, or the laymen Muslims have upon their Muslim brothers.

That being said, do not be of those who voluntarily bring heartache or sadness to Muslims, by sharing with them slander against them, that they might otherwise be ignorant of occurring. Even the Prophet     was hurt by the slander/Kufr of Quraysh, Allah   Says:

      

Imam Ibn Jarir At-Tabari   comments on this Ayah and said:

The Exalted says  in His mention  to His Prophet Muhammad : And indeed We know, O Muhammad, that your chest is constricted by what these polytheists of your people say  by their denial of you, their mockery of you, and their mockery of what you have brought to them.

Its also mentioned by Ibn Abee ad-Dunya in his Al-Ishraf:

tim ibn Yay narrated to us, saying: I heard Muammad ibn Yay al-Marwaz say: A man said to Wahb ibn Munabbih, So-and-so has insulted you. He replied: Could the devil find no messenger other than you?

And these are widely cited although I couldnt find a Sanad for them, although due to its meaning, it is worthy of mentioning as a reminder for us all, InshaAllah:

Its said that a man came to a wise man and said: So-and-so has insulted you. He replied: He shot an arrow at me and it did not reach me  so why did you carry the arrow and lodge it in my heart?

And its said that a man came to al-Shfi  may Allah have mercy on him  and said: So-and-so speaks ill of you. He replied: If you are telling the truth, then you are a tale-carrier (Nammm); and if you are lying, then you are a transgressor. So the man was ashamed and left.

Defend your brothers in Islam, without hesitation, but be weary not fall into the trap of Nameemah unintentionally, lest you hurt your brother(s) unintentionally. If anything, if someone gets slandered often, be the bearer of good and positive news instead. Work to spread their Dawah, and make it widespread. Share with them the messages of support that flood social media during the slander, instead of the slander itself. 

Theres a wonderful website  thats been assembled, Allahumma Brek. Every time someone slanders the Shaykh, share this website. Let the hypocrites die in their rage."

Text:
"How the Najdi scholars refuted the extremists

In the name of Allah, the Most Merciful, the Bestower of Mercy.

From: Abd al-Laf ibn Abd al-Ramn ibn asan
To: Abd al-Azz al-Khab

Peace be upon those who follow guidance, and upon the righteous servants of Allah.

To proceed:

I have read your letter and understood its contents, and what you intended by way of apology. However, you erred in your statement that what our shaykh, the father, denounced, namely your declaring the people of truth to be disbelievers, and your belief that you are correct, when you denied it.

You mentioned that your brothers from al-Naq are disputing with you and are arguing with you concerning us, and that they say that we have been silent regarding certain matters. And you know that they often mention this to attack our creed and to criticize our methodology. Even if they do not explicitly make Takfeer, they have approached its borders. So we seek refuge in Allah from misguidance after being upon guidance, from deviating away from the path of rectitude, and from blindness.

I witnessed, in the year 64, two men resembling you who were among the renegades in al-As. They abandoned the Friday prayer and the congregational prayer, and declared the Muslims of that land to be disbelievers. Their argument was similar to yours. They said: The people of al-As sit with Ibn Fayrz and mix with him and others like him, those who did not reject the ght by disbelieving in it, nor explicitly declare his grandfather to be a disbeliever who rejected the call of Shaykh Muammad, and did not accept it, and instead opposed it.

They both said: Whoever does not explicitly declare him to be a disbeliever is himself a disbeliever in Allah and has not rejected the ght by disbelieving in it. And whoever sits with him is like him. And upon these two false and misguided premises, they built rulings implying clear apostasy, such that they even abandoned returning greetings of Salam.

Their matter was raised to me, so I summoned them, threatened them, and spoke harshly to them. At first, they claimed that they were upon the creed of Shaykh Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb, and that his letters were with them. So I clarified their errors and refuted their misconceptions with what I had in that gathering.

I informed them that Shaykh Muammad is free from this belief and this path, and that he does not declare anyone to be a Kaafir except for those matters that the Muslims have unanimously agreed upon that makes one a Kaafir, such as major shirk, or rejecting the verses of Allah and His messengers, or any of that, after the proof has been shown to him and is properly conveyed. Such as making Takfeer on the one who worships the righteous, prays to them alongside Allah, and for making them His rivals in what He alone deserves in worship and authority over the creation.

This is agreed upon by the people of knowledge and faith. Every group among the followers of the various upheld Mazhabs has devoted a major chapter to this issue, mentioning its rulings, and that which causes apostasy and its implications, and explicitly mentioning Shirk. Ibn ajar even singled out this issue in a book he titled: al-Ilm bi-Qawi al-Islm (Informing about the Nullifiers of Islam).

These two Persians later showed repentance and remorse and claimed that the truth had become clear to them. Then they went to the coast and returned to that same doctrine. It reached us that they declared the leaders of the Muslims to be Kaafirs for communicating with the Egyptian rulers. Rather, they even declared as disbelievers those among the Muslim scholars who associated with those who communicated with them. We seek refuge in Allah from misguidance after being upon guidance and from relapse after rectitude.

We have also been informed about similar matters regarding you. And that you ....

Continued  "

After mentioning this story and what Ibn Khuzaymah said about them, Imam al-Dhahabi commented: 

They are not liars; rather, they are reliable Imams. The Shaykh only spoke according to what was conveyed to him about them. So may Allah curse those who spread slander, and those who go about with namimah (tale-carrying).

(Siyar Alam al-Nubala 14/380)"

Text:
"In defense of Shaykh Ibn Jibrn by the Grand Muft, Shaykh li al-Fawzn.

Q: May Allh bless you, this questioner says that some students of knowledge were happy at the death of Shaykh Ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him. Is this action correct?

Ans: This is not permissible. Rejoicing at the death of a Muslim in general is not permissible, because he is someone who has entered the protection of Islm, so how much more at the death of a scholar? How much more at the death of a scholar! It is not permissible for them to rejoice at the death of a scholar from the scholars of the Muslims. This only happens either out of ignorance, or because of hypocrisy, and we seek refuge with Allh. Yes.

Q: May Allh preserve you, O Shaykh, we see now on some social media platforms that some brothers, may Allh guide them, we see them speaking against major scholars such as Shaykh Abdullh ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, and Shaykh Abd al-Ramn al-Barrk, may Allh preserve him and prolong his life, attributing to them things Allh has sent down no authority for. They follow up their slips and say, These people said such-and-such, so their intention must be such-and-such. What is your guidance, may Allh preserve you, especially since they have many followers on these platforms?

Ans: This is not permissible at all. This is backbiting, from the forbidden backbiting, and backbiting the scholars is even more severe, and we seek refuge with Allh. It is not permissible to backbite the scholars or speak against them or follow up their mistakes. You must advise such people if you see them, or if any one of them is among you, then advise him regarding this. As the scholars have said: The flesh of the scholars is poisoned, their flesh is poisoned. So it is not permissible to backbite them or speak against them.

Q: May Allh be good to you, this question has been repeated often: there are those who attack some of the shaykhs, such as Shaykh Abdullh ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, and describe him with inappropriate descriptions. What is your direction for such people, and what is your advice for those who have made wounding the scholars and supplicators their occupation?

Ans: May Allh be good to you, these people harm no one but themselves; they do not harm the mashyakh. They harm none but themselves. Even if we suppose that some of the shaykhs have some error, if he is alive, he is advised; and if he is dead, he is asked forgiveness for, because he is your brother. You seek forgiveness for him and ask Allh to grant him forgiveness, reward, and recompense. Yes. May Allh be good to you.
 
Source:
 

We advise our brothers to fear Allh and to stop circulating claims that are baseless. They should also avoid exaggerating the status of certain individuals by giving them titles they neither merit nor have attained. This kind of ghuluw is harmful, and it only creates the false impression that the one making criticism is a scholar of tremendous authority, when that is simply not the case. Shaykh Muqbil, may Allh reward him for what he contributed, had his efforts in the field of adth and his role in reviving study among many students. However, he was not free of shortcomings, and it is well-known that his knowledge in usl al-fiqh, fiqh, and the broader Islamic sciences was limited compared to the major scholars of his time. Therefore, calling him Shaykh al-Islm is an exaggeration without evidence. In truth, Shaykh Ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, was far ahead of him in jurisprudence, its principles, and in many other fields of knowledge. May Allh honor our scholars, reward them for their efforts, forgive their mistakes, and protect us from statements that cause division or unnecessary hostility within the Salaf community. mn."

Text:
"It these quburists, who claim to be from Ahl al-Sunna, read some books they would realize that it was Allh who revived the dead after Eis (peace be upon him) prayed to Allh for it.

Imm al-abar said:

Eis (peace be upon him) revived the deceased by praying to Allh for them; thus, his prayer was answered.

| Tafsr al-abar, vol. 5, p. 424.

Even the Ashar scholar, Fakhr al-Deen al-Rz, said:

Is it permissible to say that He (Allh the Most High) placed within the self of Eis (peace be upon him) a characteristic in the sense that whenever he blew into something his blowing would necessitate for that thing to become alive? Or to say that it isnt like that; but rather, Allh, the Most High, used to create life within that body with His ability at the moment when Eis (peace be upon him) blew into it as a way of manifesting a miracle? This second explanation is the truth because Allh, the Most High, said: The One who created death and life (67:2). And it was mentioned that Ibrhm (peace be upon him) said in his debate with the king: My Lord is the One who gives life and cause death (2:258), so if this characteristic occurred for other than Him then that argument would have perished.

| al-Tafsr al-Kabr, by Fakhr al-Deen al-Rz, vol. 8, pp. 228 229.

Its essentially the same as the story mentioned in the following verse:

So We said: "Strike him (the dead man) with a piece of it (the cow)." Thus Allh brings the dead to life and shows you HisAyt(proofs, evidence, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) so that you may understand.

| Qurn [Hilali & Khan Translation], ch. 2, v. 73.

"Jam'at Ahl as-Sunnah lid-Da'wah wa'l-Jihd (JASDJ), mostly known as Boko Haram, recently released a video threatening ISWAP. The group in the video appears to be based in the Lake Chad area under the authority of the JAS overall leader Bakura Doro, aka Abu Umaymah.    

The speaker begins by calling ISWAP apostates and deviants, and tells them not to rejoice over the death of Abu Bakr al-Shekawi (Shekau). As a reminder, Shekau blew himself up in 2021 when he was cornered by ISWAP in the Sambisa Forest. ISWAP had called upon him with loudspeakers to surrender, but he refused.    

He adds that after Shekau there are many other Shekaus who dont die and dont change, and who follow his ways and teachings, and will remain until the Day of Judgement.    

He calls ISWAP deviants for not considering regular Muslim civilians apostates for living in territories controlled by the Nigerian government, which JAS describes as the general of the polytheists. This is a major difference between JAS (Boko Haram) and ISWAP, as the latter only sees the military, their auxiliaries, and Christians who dont pay the jizyah as legitimate targets. By contrast, JAS views everyone living under the apostate governments as legitimate targets, which is the reason behind the recent bombings in Maiduguri by JAS.    

The speaker then addresses ISWAP again, urging them to repent before JAS destroys them, and says that if they do, they will be safe from them.  

He then greets JAS fighters in all their provinces, the Mandara Mountains, and Sadikus mobile faction in the northwest and north central, as they renew their pledge of allegiance to Abu Umaymah. Children are visible among the group."

Text:
"NEW: The confession of the perpetrator of the Tadamon Massacre, Amjad Yousesf:

 The individual appearing in the viral footage known as the Tadamon massacre is me, Amjad Youssef. These events date back to the early years of the Syrian revolution. 

 At the time, I was accompanied by Najib al-Halabi and a photographer whose name I cannot recall. We rounded up 40 individuals at random, under the pretext that they were 'terrorists'.

 We took them to a pre-dug pit, where I began executing them, as shown in the video. In some instances, I waited for the victim to fall into the pit before firing; in others, I shot them before they fell. 

 Intermittently, I would call on Najib al-Halabi to take his turn in the killing, until we had executed everyone present.

 We had previously placed car tires at the bottom of the pit. After the executions, we piled more tires over the bodies and set them on fire to prevent the scent of decomposition from spreading throughout the area. We then filled the pit. 

 I received no military orders to carry out this massacre, it was my own personal decision. 

 I cannot explain why there were exactly 40 victims, the selection was arbitrary, dictated solely by the circumstances at the time.

Source: Usman A Khan and translation by Syrian journalist Qusay Noor"

Text:
"Regarding Pakistan / Afghanistan relations 

From my friend @LongshotIbrahim -- a brother who has written on and studied the subject considerably:

I have mentioned this before but in 1980s/1990s various Afghan factions, incl both Taliban and some of their foes, supported Pakistan and Kashmir and went out of the way to do so. This was while an increasingly pro-India US was running interference for India.

While Afg mujahideen in general backed Pakistan and Kashmir, outstanding case was Hikmatyar (Afg prime minister in 1993-94, then 1995-96), and his field commander Khalid Faruqi. Hizbul Mujahideen were trained and at least partly modelled on them for a decade or so.

Another major case was Arsala-Rahmani Daulat (prime minister 1994-95), who later joined Taliban and was murdered in 2012. A third was justice minister Jalaluddin Haqqani, father of today's Taliban interior minister Sirajuddin.

In general Taliban remained pro-Pakistan thru their first emirate. During the 1999 Pak war w India over Kashmir, Taliban prime minister Mohammad Rabbani offered to send thousands of fighters (but was declined bcos there wasnt really a manpower shortage).

At the end of 1999, Pak militants related to Kashmir jihad incl. Masood Azhar were released from Indian custody in a hostage exchange mediated, much to Pakistani glee, by Taliban Kandahar corps commander Akhtar Usmani.

Many of Kashmir-focused mujahideen also supported Taliban in late 1990s incl Saifullah Akhtar and AbdulJabbar Farooq. They fought directly under defence minister Ubaidullah Akhund and his lieutenant, today's Afghan security minister Ibrahim Sadar.

In east Afg, Taliban and Hizb and also Salafi networks were linked to Kashmir-focused mujahideen incl Salafi "Lashkar Taiba". One commander in Kunar who latee fought US at the famed Korengal valley, AbdulRahim, was a Hizbi turned Taliban but of Salafi persuasion, close to Taiba.

In 2001-02 after US invasion, Pakistan banned some of these groups (but tacitly tolerated others like Taiba). AbdulJabbar and Saifullah as well as Ilyas Kashmiri revolted against Pakistan, while Khalil and Azhar nervously walked a fine line.

In 2004, partly bcos of these rebels but also largely to distract US after Iran/Libya tattled, Pakistani army invaded Waziristan region. The army was surprised at local tribal backlash and adopted the line "Why are yoy fighting us, go and fight US in Afghanistan"

This line was supported by Afghan Taliban and practically every one of their leaders - emir Umar, Jalaluddin and Sirajuddin, Usmani, Daddullah Lang, Ubaidullah, chief justice NurMohammad Saqib, Mohammad Yasir, Sadar Azam - mediated on Pakistan's behalf with Waziristan insurgency.

However US intelligence (CIA) infiltrated Pakistan in 2005, using earthquake relief and humanitarian aid as a cover. Their targets were not only Taliban or Qaeda but also Pakistani groups linked to Kashmir, such as Taiba

Since the late 1990s US stance on Kashmir had been "Pakistan should forget Kashmir, make peace with India, and focus on its own Islamic radicalism", and this is what US (and their local lapdogs in Pakistan) pursued.

US also repeatedly sabotaged deals and ceasefires that, mediated by Taliban, army had made with insurgency in Waziristan. Ie it was US, not Taliban, who put Pakistan army and civilians at risk; it was US, not Taliban, who undermined Kashmir issue

Despite this Pakistan scrambled to appease and placate US, who would constantly bark "Do more" and undermine Pakistan further. It wasnt until 2011 that CIA agents were expelled as US/Pak relations hit a low and by then US was cracking down on Kashmir activists too

Meanwhile despite the Pak/US relations various Taliban networks incl Sirajuddin Haqqani continued to hit India; e.g. in 2008 Indian attache Ravi Mehta was killed at Kabul in a joint op w/ Taiba.

Sirajuddin was particularly hated by US for his role in a "triple agent" trick that killed CIA field commander Jennifer Matthews in what is still regarded as one of the most humiliating episodes in CIA history.

One CIA agent from this period - when agency was systematically destabilizing Pakistan, vilifying Kashmir, and bolstering India - was Sarah Adams, who has a particular visceral hatred for Sirajuddin Haqqani even by the agency's standards

Now bear in mind Adams' background. She was working for CIA in region at a time when it was actively involved in what even the most charitable appraisal could call anti-Pakistan/anti-Kashmir activity, during a period when Taliban were doing what Pakistan requested incl. v India.

however, after 2022 Pak coup - which was at least partly done for US approval - Pakistani pseudo-nationalists have embraced Adams as an unimpeachable source whenever she blames Taliban for anything, bcos they are eager to offload any responsibility onto "those ingrate Afghans"

Adams blames Taliban for everything, and she does this in large part bcos of her background and her resentment at Haqqanis, in particular. In 2023 she claimed that Sirajuddin had masterminded Hamas/Hezbollah war v Israel (oh, of course she also hates Palestine & loves Israel)

Adams doesn't give a shit abt Kashmir. She is pro-Israel, and Israel is pro-India, and India is anti-Kashmir. Her CIA career was spent partly in pro-India/anti-Kashmir activity. However, she knows that Pakistanis care about Kashmir and she wants to turn Pakistanis against Taliban

so after pro-US 2022 coup in Pakistan, Adams gets onto podcasts and "exposes" her target: "Oh, Taliban betrayed Kashmiris; oh, Taliban backstabbed Pakistan". If she could, she'd accuse Taliban of murdering Liaquat Ali-Khan in 1951 in order to whip up anti-Afghan sentiment in Pak

The fact is Taliban have a long record of supporting Pakistan and even Kashmir, and a looong record of opposing India at least on this issue. Whereas US,and its agents like Adams, have exactly the opposite record. But to justify current Pak war v Afg, she spreads these daft tales

Also, Taliban government is more or less the same ppl, with a handful of younger faces, as it was in 1996-2001 when they supported Pakistan. None of these people were anti-Pakistan until Pakistan, after the 2022 coup, started bombarding them."

Text:
"Salafis do not believe that the pagans of Makkah possessed a complete understanding of Tawheed al-Rububiyyah. When they assert that the pagans affirmed Tawheed al-Rububiyyah, they are referring to its foundational aspect (asl), which is the belief in one God as the ultimate creator and sustainer of the universe, the only being who is entirely independent. Salafis emphasize this point in response to saint venerators who argue that they are not guilty of shirk simply because they do not consider their saints to be independent of Allah. Salafis contend that the independence of a being is not the sole criterion for determining whether certain beliefs or actions constitute major shirk.

Salafis are well aware that the pagan Quraysh, while acknowledging Allah as the supreme creator, also attributed daughters to Him, worshipped idols, and failed to affirm His attributes correctly. These theological errors are considered distortions of Tawheed al-Rububiyyah.

Prominent Salafi scholars, such as Shaykh Abdulrahman Al-Barrak and Shaykh Yusuf Al-Ghafees, have argued that saint venerators also commit shirk in Rububiyyah by ascribing certain powers to their saints, powers that should be attributed exclusively to Allah.

To avoid semantic confusion, if Rububiyyah is primarily understood as Allahs independence and self-sufficiency, then Salafis assert that most of the pagan Quraysh acknowledged this foundational aspect. (However, Salafi scholars such as Shaykh Sultan al-Umayri, Abdulrazzaq Abu Badr, and Abu Bakr Muhammad Zakariyyah note that the Quraysh were not a homogeneous group and differed in their beliefs.) If Rububiyyah is defined in broader terms, while still rooted in the foundation of Allahs independence, Salafis maintain that the pagans only affirmed this foundational aspect.

The central issue, according to Salafis, is the affirmation of Allahs independence and the rejection of any justification by saint venerators that their actions do not constitute major shirk."
