Text:
"Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar    was among the most devoted, scrupulous, and steadfast Companions of the Messenger of Allh . He was a man whose life became a living embodiment of the Sunnah, so precise in his adherence that he would follow even the smallest actions of the Prophet  out of love and reverence.

He was Abdullh ibn Umar ibn al-Khab al-Adaw al-Qurash, the son of the great Caliph Sayyidun Umar ibn al-Khab   . He was born in Makkah before the Hijrah and embraced Islm at a young age alongside his noble father. He emigrated to al-Madnah al-Munawwarah at the age of ten, and thus was counted among the early Muhjirn, those whom Allh, Most High, praised in His Book:

And the foremost, the first of the Muhjirn and the Anr and those who followed them with excellence, Allh is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him, and He has prepared for them gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever. That is the great success.

[Qurn 9:100]

In his youth, he longed to participate in battle alongside the Messenger of Allh . He was presented at Badr but was turned back due to his young age. He was again presented at Uud, when he was only thirteen years old, hoping to join the ranks, but he was sent back by the Prophet  due to his youth, alongside Sayyidun Usmah ibn Zayd   . That moment weighed heavily upon him, and he later said: 

A night like that never came my way, filled with sleeplessness, sadness, and weeping, because the Messenger of Allh  did not accept me.

After returning him from Uud, the Messenger of Allh  appointed him among those tasked with guarding the children in Madnah. 

Yet his heart remained attached to striving in the path of Allh, and his sincere supplication to be permitted to fight was soon answered. In the fifth year after Hijrah, when he reached the age of fifteen, he was allowed to participate in the Battle of al-Khandaq. 

From that point forward, he took part in the major campaigns of Islm. He participated in the campaign against Ban Qurayah, the journey to al-udaybiyyah, and was among those who pledged beneath the tree in the Pledge of al-Riwn, a moment honored by Allh, Most High:

Indeed, Allh was pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you under the tree. He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down tranquility upon them and rewarded them with a near victory.

[Qurn 48:18]

He later joined the campaigns of Khaybar, Mutah, the Conquest of Makkah, the expedition against Ban Judhaymah, the Battle of unayn, and the march to Tabk. He was also among the soldiers of the army of Sayyidun Usmah ibn Zayd   . 

After returning from this expedition, he volunteered in the wars against apostasy and had the honor of participating in the Battle of Yammah against Musaylamah al-Kadhdhb. 

He later marched to Shm and fought at al-Yarmk, then joined the campaigns in the Persian lands under Sayyidun Sad ibn Ab Waqq   , attending al-Qdisiyyah, Jall, and the conquest of al-Madin. He also took part in the conquest of Egypt and campaigns in North Africa. 

Even in his later years, he joined the expedition toward Constantinople, about which the Prophet  said: The first army to attack the city of Caesar is forgiven.

What distinguished Ibn Umar    above all was his extraordinary commitment to following the Sunnah. He would carefully observe where the Prophet  prayed, where he sat, and even the paths he walked, then imitate them with remarkable precision. 

If he saw the Prophet  perform prayer in a particular place, he would later pray in that very same place. If he saw him make supplication while standing, he would supplicate while standing; and if he saw him invoke Allh while sitting, he would do the same. His life was not merely one of narration, but of complete imitation.

He was among the foremost scholars of the Companions and a prolific narrator of hadith, transmitting over 2,600 narrations. His knowledge was paired with deep caution, he was known for his reluctance to issue legal verdicts without certainty, and he preferred silence over speculation. 

Due to his age, knowledge, and unwavering adherence to the Sunnah, he became known in his time as the Shaykh of the abah, and even powerful governors such as al-ajjj ibn Ysuf treated him with respect and deference due to his immense status amongst the Muslims. From the greatest testimonies to his precision in transmitting the Sunnah is that he stands at the head of one of the most authentic chains in hadith, the Golden Chain.

Mlik  Nfi  Ibn Umar.

A generation of leading Tbin took knowledge from him, including Sad ibn al-Musayyib, Thbit al-Bunn, ws, al-Zuhr, Muammad ibn Srn, Nfi (his freed servant), Mujhid, and many others. His influence thus extended deeply into the next generation of scholarship. 

His legacy was also carried forward through his own household, as several of his sons became scholars, most notably Slim ibn Abdullh ibn Umar, who was counted among the great jurists of Madnah. Through both his students and his family, his knowledge and methodology continued to shape the transmission of the Sunnah for generations.

During the periods of civil strife, having lived to witness both the First and Second Fitnah among the Muslims, Ibn Umar    consistently adopted a principled stance of restraint. He presented himself at the sittings of arbitration, demonstrating his desire for unity among the Muslims. 

He famously said: When you call me to prayer, I respond. However, when you call me to fight a Muslim, I do not. He never raised his sword against the believers. Yet, on the other hand, he answered every call to strive against the disbelievers, embodying the words of Allh, Most High:

Humble toward the believers, powerful against the disbelievers; they strive in the cause of Allh 

[Qurn 5:54]

He was known for his humility, generosity, and detachment from worldly life. Wealth would come to him, yet he would spend it freely in charity. He freed many slaves and cared deeply for the poor and needy. His nights were often spent in prayer, and his days in fasting and remembrance.

Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar    passed away in Makkah in 73 AH and was buried near Dh uw, leaving behind a legacy of unwavering adherence to the Sunnah and a life defined by sincerity and caution.

May Allh be pleased with Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar   , the devoted follower of the Sunnah and one of the greatest scholars among the Companions of the Messenger of Allh ."

Text:
"Shaykh li al-Fawzn speaks about those who allow the disbelievers into the lands of muslims when assisting them against the muslims and how this is disbelief even if he hates their religion.

Interestingly, he then speaks about those who bring them in and enabled them to build churches and the likes, and this is what he says :

_

Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al Wahhb said: [That if someone openly displays their] agreement with their religion [of the Mushrikin]

Shaykh li al-Fawzn said: Meaning: He enabled them [the mushrikin] to establish churches in the lands of the Muslims, and granted them freedom, and said to the Muslims: Do not prevent them from anything; whatever they request from you, comply with it. This is mudhanah, because our religion forbids this.



Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb said: [And if he does this then] indeed he is a disbeliever like them.

Shaykh li al-Fawzn commentates: Because he enabled the [religion of the] disbelievers and was pleased with their religion, and he forewent what the religion of Islm obliged upon him  namely disassociating the religion of the disbelievers and disavowing it. He did not disavow it; rather, he permitted it, justified it, and granted them freedom [to establish their religion] in the lands of the Muslims.

Shaykh Sulaymn ibn Abd Allh ibn Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb said: [And if he does this then indeed he is a disbeliever like them] even if he detests their religion and hates them.

Shaykh li al-Fawzn commentates: Meaning: if he loves their religion, then this makes him a disbeliever (kfir), an apostate, if he establish them. And if he establishes them while he hates their religion, then this act of his constitutes apostasy from Islm, because he has assisted them against the Muslims.

        (31,32)",

Text:
"So we rejected this statement and said: this is a dangerous statement. Its implication is that you are nullifying the Fatw of the scholars in Nawzil. If you see that there is a mistake, and you are a Salaf who respects the scholars and also respects the rulers, then write to them. Present your viewpoint only. But to establish this as a general principlethis is false and wrong! 

This resembles the approach of the Surrs and Qubs during the Gulf crisis, who said: Our scholars do not understand current affairswe understand current affairs. America entered, Europe did such-and-such, and you, O scholarslike ibn Bzyou are only with the rulers, obeying them. The same wordsonly the people and circumstances have changed. There it was a Gulf crisis related to the entry of disbelievers (to the lands of the Muslims); here it is a COVID crisis related to medicine.

Furthermore, this man (making these claims) works in medicine. If we look at his specialization, do you work in every field of medicine and every specialty? No. Then speak only within your level. That is one point.

Secondly, do you want the entire Ummah to abandon the doctors of the world and the consultants, and instead follow your opinion? This is dangerous speech! It is unclear what this person intends with such statements. 

Despite that, he has been advised, and we have advised him before. We even sent to him years ago when he issued a Fatw. Look at this Fatwmany people may not even be aware of it. He issued a ruling that social distancing was Minor Shirk. That means Al-Fawzn has fallen into Shirk, the Muft has fallen into Shirk, and our scholars such as Ubayd and others have fallen into Shirk. And we, the students of knowledge, have all fallen into Shirk. And the Fatw of the scholars who permitted distancing are, according to him, Fatw of Shirk.

So who is behind you that you speak with such words? What do you want, that you strike at the scholars by claiming they have fallen into (minor) Shirk, that they should not be referred to, and that they neither understand nor comprehend? This is what he says in the audio recordings. 

Some have not even listened to these recordings yet and do not want to listenand this is a calamity. You find a person plunging headlong into Fitnah while knowing nothing. And if you say to him, Come, let us sit and clarify things for you, he refuses to listen. This is blind partisanship. 

As for us, we clarify the error. Whoever is guided, then all praise is due to Allh, and whoever goes astray, then it is upon himself.

Shaykh Abdul-Azz ibn Bz was asked on his official site: Should we refer only to doctors, or to the scholars? He replied: No. One must return to the scholar. If the matter were purely medical, then yeswe would return to the doctors; because they are the people of specialization. But this concerns a matter connected to medicine and also to the Sharah.

Does Al-Fawzn immediately issue a ruling when a purely medical issue comes to him? You think highly of yourself and consider yourself an expert. But when this scholarAl-Fawzn, the Muft, Al-Abbd, Rab, and othersare asked about a worldly technical matter such as medicine or agriculture, do they refer to specialists or not? They do refer to the specialists. This shows that you do not understand how the scholars issue Fatw.

I have already explained this in detail in one of the refutationsthe methodology of the scholar in giving Fatwand it is published on my channel. Yet some people ignore all of this and do not want to listen. 

Continued"

Text:
"Somali and foreign IS-Somalia leadership still remain entrenched in the Puntland mountains.

As shown in recently released ISIS media, the footage features Abu Ammar al-Maghribi, a senior figure within ISIS-Somalia, who was present in the Cal Miskaad mountain range in the Bari region. For some time, conflicting reports suggested that much of the groups leadership had withdrawn from these mountains following clashes with Puntland security forces and U.S. airstrikes. However, credible intelligence indicates that the core leadership of ISIS-Somalia remains in place.

In particular, Abdulkadir Mumin, the groups leader, is believed to still be operating in the area alongside several of his closest associates. It is also assessed that hundreds of foreign fighters remain in the region, determined to continue fighting despite heavy losses. Many have been killed, while others have surrendered or been captured, including some now in the custody of Puntland authorities.

Stay tuned and follow @Wargelinn for further updates.

Text:
"THIS IS NOT THE END

Shaykh Suliman al Alwaan (may Allah free him) said:

The fall of Dr al-Salm, Baghdad, and other Islamic lands is not the end of the world nor the end of Islam. Let the gloaters be disgraced. The future belongs to this deen, and soon the last part of this Ummah will be joined to its earliest part. So we must not weaken, nor should our efforts cease in supporting Muslims everywhere. Allah has praised those who face trials and hardship with steadfastness and patience. He said: And when the believers saw the confederates, they said: This is what Allah and His Messenger promised us, and Allah and His Messenger spoke the truth. And it increased them only in faith and submission.

These trials and hardships that befall the Muslims are a test, a means to distinguish the wicked from the good, the righteous from the corrupt, and the truthful from the liar. The believers increase in faith, while the hypocrites and those who worship Allah on the edge (I.e. in doubt) weaken, fall into cowardice, and show gloating. They say, If they had obeyed us, they would not have been killed. Allah the Exalted said: And among the people is he who worships Allah on an edge. If good befalls him, he is content with it; but if a trial touches him, he turns back on his face. He loses both this world and the Hereafter, that is the clear loss.

And He said: And among the people is he who says, We believe in Allah, but when he is harmed in Allahs cause, he considers the trial of the people as the punishment of Allah. But if victory comes from your Lord, they will surely say, Indeed, we were with you. Is not Allah most knowing of what is within the breasts of all beings?

And upon those whom Allah has entrusted with this great responsibility, such as the imams of mosques and others, is to urge the worshippers toward piety, repentance to Allah, giving them glad tidings of victory and empowerment, and making them aware of what the spiteful West intends, and what their brothers among the hypocrites, secularists, and the like are planning: a comprehensive assault on Islam and the Muslims, as they have openly declared a crusade.

They must work by every possible means to halt their advance and repel their aggression against the sanctities, wealth, and honor of the Muslims, and to strive against them with wealth, lives, and speech. For the Prophet  said: "Strive against the Mushrikeen with your wealth, your lives, and your tongues."
Reported by Imam Ahmad in his Musnad (12246), Ab Dwd (2504), and al-Nas (3098) through the narration of Hammad ibn Salamah, from Humayd, from Anas. Its chain is sound.

Text:
"The Danger of Free-Mixing and a Warning to Those Who Treat the Matter Lightly

Ab Hurayrah narrated that the Messenger of Allh (   ) said:

The best rows for men are the first rows, and the worst are the last rows, and the best rows for women are the last rows, and the worst are the first rows. Muslim 440.

Al-Nawaw comments:

The reason the last rows are preferred for women who are present with men is because they are farther from mixing with the men, seeing them, becoming attached in the heart due to seeing their movements or hearing their speech, and similar matters. For this reason, the first rows are considered the opposite (less preferable) for women, and Allh knows best.

If this concern applies even in the most sacred of places, where hearts are softened, desires are restrained by the fear of Allh, and one is surrounded by righteous believers, yet unnecessary mixing between men and women is still cautioned against, then what about places of lesser sanctity, where temptation is stronger, heedlessness is more common, and restrain is weaker?

Munb al-ml"

Text:
"The Difference Between Dr al-Islm and Dr al-Kufr:

Ab Ysuf, the companion of Ab anfah, said:

A land is considered Dr al-Islm when the rulings of Islam are manifest in it, even if most of its inhabitants are disbelievers. And a land is considered Dr al-Kufr when the rulings of disbelief are manifest in it, even if most of its inhabitants are Muslims.
[al-Mabs by al-Sarakhs 10/144]

Ibn Mufli al-anbal said:

Every land in which the rulings of the Muslims prevail is Dr al-Islm, and if the rulings of disbelief prevail in it, then it is Dr al-Kufr. There is no third category beyond these two.
[al-db al-Shariyyah 1/213]

Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah said:

The majority said: Dr al-Islm is that land in which the Muslims reside and upon which the rulings of Islam are implemented. Whatever the rulings of Islam are not implemented upon is not Dr al-Islm, even if it is adjacent to it.
[Akm Ahl al-Dhimmah 1/266]

Al-Q Ab Yal al-Farr said:

Every land in which the dominance is for the rulings of disbelief over the rulings of Islam is Dr al-Kufr.
[al-Mutamad f Ul al-Dn, p. 276]

Al-Ksn said:

When the rulings of disbelief become manifest in a land, it becomes Dr al-Kufr; a land becomes Dr al-Kufr by the manifestation of the rulings of disbelief.
[Badi al-ani 9/4375]

Muammad ibn al-asan al-Shaybn said:

Every land is attributed either to Islam or to disbelief. A land is attributed to Islam when its rulings are implemented in it, and it is attributed to disbelief when its rulings are implemented in it. Just as one says Paradise is the abode of peace and Hell is the abode of ruin, due to the presence of peace in Paradise and ruin in Hell. Likewise, the manifestation of Islam or disbelief is by the manifestation of their rulings.
[Badi al-ani 9/4375]

Manr al-Buht said:

Hijrah is obligatory upon one who is unable to manifest his religion in Dr al-arb, which is the land where the rulings of disbelief prevail.
[Kashshf al-Qin 3/43]

Text:
"The school of dilution and adaptation 

Question:
What do you mean by the dilution (tamyee) and adaptation (tatwee) that you mentioned?

Shaykh Abu Yahya al-Libi (may Allah have mercy upon him) said:

This approach is one of the afflictions that has befallen our Islamic Ummah in this age, and many have been tested by it, those described as thinkers, enlightened individuals, moderates, or centrists.

The reality of this methodology is the dilution of the truths of the deen and their reshaping and subjugation so that they conform to understandings, perspectives, and convictions that its proponents have adopted, especially in matters where they feel there is conflict or tension with ideas promoted by Westerners.

So you find these adapters sparing no effort and leaving no means unused to try to prove that Islam agrees with Western ideas, matches their rulings, or even preceded them in what they have proposed.

Unfortunately, this contagion has affected many Muslim scholars and preachers, to the point that some of them have become leaders of this school, those scholars upon whom Allah placed the responsibility of conveying His message to people as it is, without distortion or deception, and who should not be swayed by desire or fear.

As Allah the Exalted said:

Those who convey the messages of Allah and fear Him, and do not fear anyone except Allah. And sufficient is Allah as a Reckoner.

This school of dilution and adaptation has made all matters of the deen into an open domain, freely entered by their intellects, polluted by their ideas, and subjected to their research, without any restraint or trustworthiness holding them back.

This is a great calamity of our time.

The Quran has already clarified this reality and exposed the inner motives of such people, namely deviation and corruption of the heart, which can be summarized as infatuation with Western culture, and being carried away by intellectual temptation.

Allah says:

As for those in whose hearts is deviation, they follow what is ambiguous, seeking Fitna and seeking its interpretation.

The Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) recited this verse and then said:

If you see those who follow what is ambiguous, they are the ones Allah has named, so beware of them.

Therefore, a Muslim, so that his deen remains sound and he protects himself from deviation and getting lost in paths of misguidance, must beware of such people and not be deceived by names, titles, or fame.

What they are doing is polluting our Islamic sources and corrupting our pure concepts, which Islam was keen to preserve in their independence, in terminology, meanings, and substance, so that no impurity would enter and disturb this pure spring.

In this regard, there is the incident when the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) was approached by Umar (may Allah be pleased with him), who said:
We hear statements from the Jews that we find appealing, should we write some of them down?

The Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) replied:

Text:
"This is my will and my final message. If these words reach you, know that Israel has succeeded in killing me and silencing my voice. First, peace be upon you and Allahs mercy and blessings.

Allah knows I gave every effort and all my strength to be a support and a voice for my people, ever since I opened my eyes to life in the alleys and streets of the Jabalia refugee camp. My hope was that Allah would extend my life so I could return with my family and loved ones to our original town of occupied Asqalan (Al-Majdal). But Allahs will came first, and His decree is final. I have lived through pain in all its details, tasted suffering and loss many times, yet I never once hesitated to convey the truth as it is, without distortion or falsificationso that Allah may bear witness against those who stayed silent, those who accepted our killing, those who choked our breath, and whose hearts were unmoved by the scattered remains of our children and women, doing nothing to stop the massacre that our people have faced for more than a year and a half.

I entrust you with Palestinethe jewel in the crown of the Muslim world, the heartbeat of every free person in this world. I entrust you with its people, with its wronged and innocent children who never had the time to dream or live in safety and peace. Their pure bodies were crushed under thousands of tons of Israeli bombs and missiles, torn apart and scattered across the walls.

I urge you not to let chains silence you, nor borders restrain you. Be bridges toward the liberation of the land and its people, until the sun of dignity and freedom rises over our stolen homeland. I entrust you to take care of my family. I entrust you with my beloved daughter Sham, the light of my eyes, whom I never got the chance to watch grow up as I had dreamed.

I entrust you with my dear son Salah, whom I had wished to support and accompany through life until he grew strong enough to carry my burden and continue the mission.

I entrust you with my beloved mother, whose blessed prayers brought me to where I am, whose supplications were my fortress and whose light guided my path. I pray that Allah grants her strength and rewards her on my behalf with the best of rewards.

I also entrust you with my lifelong companion, my beloved wife, Umm Salah (Bayan), from whom the war separated me for many long days and months. Yet she remained faithful to our bond, steadfast as the trunk of an olive tree that does not bendpatient, trusting in Allah, and carrying the responsibility in my absence with all her strength and faith.

I urge you to stand by them, to be their support after Allah Almighty. If I die, I die steadfast upon my principles. I testify before Allah that I am content with His decree, certain of meeting Him, and assured that what is with Allah is better and everlasting.

O Allah, accept me among the martyrs, forgive my past and future sins, and make my blood a light that illuminates the path of freedom for my people and my family. Forgive me if I have fallen short, and pray for me with mercy, for I kept my promise and never changed or betrayed it.

Do not forget Gaza And do not forget me in your sincere prayers for forgiveness and acceptance.

Anas Jamal Al-Sharif
06.04.2025

Text:
"This video serves as an example of the importance of staying in your lane. 

      
Whoever speaks in matters external to his field ends up producing strange positions 

In this video, this speaker has ostracised millions of Muslims who trace their creed back to the time of the pious predecessors. 
He speaks as if the Salaf creed was born with Sh. Muhammad bin Abd al-Wahhb, whilst any basic student of Aqdah is aware that scholars of this creed predate Ibn Abd al-Wahhb by circa a thousand years. Even those who critique this creed and claim that it doesnt represent the Aqdah of the Salaf acknowledge the point that it predates Ibn Abdil Wahhb, citing Ibn Taymiyyahs creed as a prime example (d. 728AH). 

Furthermore, his categorisation of acceptable creeds into Ashar, Mturd and the Tahw creed is bizarre for multiple reasons. First, he argues that these creeds are the Aqidat al-Salaf, whilst ignoring & sidelining the creed of the Imm of Ahlus Sunnah, Ahmad bin Hanbal. Was his creed not the creed of the Salaf? Second, his mentioning of the Tahw creed as the third creed adds to his blunders. Both the Ashirah and the Mturdyyah would claim that the creed of al-Tahw reflects their Aqdah, as would the Athars. Specialists in this field from each of these schools do not treat the Tahw creed as a separate school of Aqdah. 

As for his immature, ridiculous comments like you have a mind of a nine-year-old and you have some IQ in your head?  they do not deserve a response. Such statements only reflect badly upon the one who says them. 

This is just a brief response; much more could be said on this mess of a video.",

Text:
"Update on Sheikh Ali Al-Timimi:

Five years ago, Dr. Ali was released into home confinement, where he remains. At that time, he had served 15 years of a life plus 70-year sentence, all of which was in solitary confinement and under SAMS. 

In the summer of 2024, the lower court finally ruled in his favour and removed the life and 30 years from his sentence, leaving approximately 25 years of the original sentence. Since then, Dr. Ali has finally been allowed to appeal the original conviction and the remaining 25 years of that sentence. 

This is the first time in US legal history that a person has had to wait 20 years to have the opportunity to appeal his convictions.

Depending on how the appellate court rules, Dr. Ali will be cleared of the charges against him or compelled to return to prison to complete the remaining 25 years.

Given that he is in his 60s, a 25-year sentence is an effective death sentence. Please keep him in your prayers and inform as many well-wishers as possible.

 Idris Palmer (@AIdrisPalmer)"

3.From heedlessness to remembrance (dhikr).
4.From desiring the worldly life to desiring the Hereafter.
5.From arrogance to humility.
6.From bad intention to genuine sincere naah.

(Ighthat al-Lahfn 1/136)

May Allah return us for many more!",

Text:
"Who was ash-Shaykh, al-Allmah, Ubayd al-Jbir?

He is the Shaykh and Allmah, Ubayd bin Abd Allh bin Sulaymn al-Jbir al-Hamadn, and Ban Jbir is among the tribes of arb of the ijz.
He was born in the town of al-Faqr in al-Far valley in the region of Madnah in 1357 AH (1938). At the age of eight, he moved with his father to Mahd al-Dhahab, where he acquired the first stages of his education. His father moved there in order to work. He remained in education at this location for eight years. After that, he returned to al-Far valley. Then in 1376 AH (1956) he settled in Madnah and, due to family circumstances, was unable to study for a period of time.

Then in the year 1381 AH (1961), he resumed his studies, starting with Dr al-adth al-Madaniyyah. Then he joined al-Mahad al-Ilm and stayed there for five years. He got married before joining the Faculty of Sharah in the Islamic University of Madnah in 1388 AH (1968). He graduated four years later in 1392 AH (1972), excelling in his grade.
From his teachers through the course of his studies include: Shaykh Sayf al-Ramn bin Amad, Shaykh Ammr bin Abd Allh, Shaykh Abd al-Ramn bin Abd Allh al-Ajln, Shaykh umayd al-zim, Shaykh ammd al-Anr and Shaykh Abd al-Musin al-Abbad.

After that, he was an imm in Masjid al-Sabt in Madnah between 1387-1392 AH. Then he was a teacher at Umar bin Abd al-Aziz Intermediate School in Jeddah from 1392 AH to 1396 AH. After that, the Shaykh worked as a caller for Markaz al-Dawah wal-Irshd, assisting the director of the centre, Shaykh Abd al-Azz bin Bz, in his presence and acting on his behalf in his absence from the end of 1396 AH to 1404 AH (1976-1983).
Then he was a teacher at the Islmic University between the end of 1407 AH to 1414 AH. During his presence there, he also attained a Masters in the subject of tafsr. After a few years serving at the Community Service Centre affiliated with the Islmic University, the Shaykh then retired in 1417 AH (1996).

After this the Shaykh continued his lectures and lessons, with students from the Islmic University frequenting his home and benefiting from his knowledge, advice and wisdom. Text:
(56)
Fond Memories 

In Dammaj, there were two main masjids: the central masjid, where all the students gathered for Sheikh Yahyas daily lessons, and another masjid located in the mazraah (farmlands). 

During Ramadhan, these two masjids would engage in a friendly competition to see who could complete the most khatmahs.

One night, during class, a note was presented to Sheikh Yahya informing him that the brothers in the mazraah had completed Surah Al-Baqarah, Aal Imran, and An-Nisaa all in a single night. After praising them and invoking blessings upon their efforts, the sheikh went on to clarify that the Prophet     recited all three of these Surahs in just one rakah.

Text:
(84) 
The Claim That Shaykh Al-Albani Was Overly Lenient and Shaykh Muqbil Overly Strict in Hadith Grading

Question: 
It is said about Shaykh Nasir Ad-Din Al-Albani that he was lenient in his authentication and weakening of hadiths, and about Shaykh Muqbil that he was overly severe. How true are these statements?

Sheikh Yahya Al-Hajoori
As for the claim that Shaykh Al-Albani was lenient in authentication and weakeningthis is incorrect. How could that be? He never weakened a hadith without providing clear evidence for its weakness. He was extremely meticulous in weakening hadiths. His book Silsilah Adh-Daifah (The Chain of Weak Hadiths) stands as a testament to this, showing his great precision. Regarding authentication, he would sometimes exercise his own reasoning (ijtihad) on certain hadiths, citing various chains and applying his own methodological principles. He might say, "This hadith, through these chains, is hasan in my view." This is similar to what Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar does in Bulugh Al-Maram, where he says, "This hadith is hasan," even though some other hadith masters or researchers may disagree. The same can be said for Az-Zaylai and other scholarsthis is a normal matter of scholarly difference.

It is true that some hadiths in Silsilah As-Sahihah (The Chain of Authentic Hadiths) which he deemed authentic are not accepted by some other scholars. However, in reality, his ijtihad was very strong, and he provided the Muslim Ummah with an immense treasure that neither a scholar nor a student can do without. He remains a mujtahid, like other mujtahids in the field of hadith. A student of knowledge should still examine the hadiths, even those found in Silsilah As-Sahihah or Irwa Al-Ghalil, and form their own conclusion based on what they see, even if it aligns with Shaykh Al-Albanis ruling. If a student feels they have not reached the level to judge a hadith as hasan or sahih as the Shaykh did, they can simply hold their own informed opinion. And all praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.

As for the claim that Shaykh Muqbil was severe in authentication and weakeningthere is no doubt that he was extremely meticulous. The "severity" here simply refers to his rigorous precision. Shaykh Muqbil would scrutinise many hadiths in light of hidden defects (ilal). I once asked him directly: "O Shaykh, the hadiths you included in As-Sahih Al-Musnad, did you examine them for hidden defects?" He replied, "I mostly examine them for hidden defects." He continued this practice throughout his life, constantly reviewing As-Sahih Al-Musnad against books of ilal. If Al-Hafidh Ad-Daraqutni, Abu Hatim, Abu Zurah, Al-Hafidh Ibn Hajar, or any of the hadith masters stated that a hadith had a defect, Shaykh Muqbil would either remove it from his collection or keep it with a note indicating its defect.

In my observation, the criticisms regarding authentication directed at our Shaykh (Muqbil), may Allah have mercy on him, are far fewer than those directed at Shaykh Al-Albani. This is worth noting, considering the vast number of hadiths that Shaykh Al-Albani ruled upon compared to the number that our Shaykh ruled upon.
Reasons_ענה-יה אך:
הנה פוסט, שהוא שיחת מחלוקת בתוך הסלפיה, עם מטרה אינפורמטיבי
לכן נראה לי אירלבנט אבל לא בטוח כאשר דמויות השיחה סלפיות תקלידיות

Text:
4. Shaking hands with non-Mahram (unrelated) women: This is an affliction that has become widespread, and no one has escaped it except those whom Allah has shown mercy. It is prohibited due to what was narrated by Abdullah bin Al-Shikhkhir who said: I heard Ma'qil bin Yasar say: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (For one of you to be stabbed in the head with an iron needle is better for him than to touch a woman who is not permissible for him.)  Narrated by Al-Tabarani in Al-Mu'jam Al-Kabeer (486).
Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And in the hadith, there is a severe threat for the one who touches a woman who is not permissible for him; thus, it contains evidence for the prohibition of shaking hands with women, because that is undoubtedly included in touching.)  As-Silsilah As-Saheehah (1/448).
> 
> (So this hadith indicates explicitly the prohibition of touching a non-Mahram woman, and from this is: shaking hands; as the Lawgiver has attached a severe threat to touching a woman who is not permissible for him, which is: being stabbed with an iron needle. Attaching a severe threat to an action indicates its prohibition.)  Al-Musarahah fi Ahkam Al-Musafahah (p.62).
> 
5. Free mixing of men and women: This often occurs in parks and recreational areas, and there is much evidence regarding the prohibition of the free mixing of men and women, so the Muslim must be wary of that. From Abu Usaid Malik bin Rabi'ah, may Allah be pleased with him, that he heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saywhile he was coming out of the mosque, and men and women had mixed in the street: (Draw back, for you must not walk in the middle of the street; keep to the sides of the street. So women used to cling to the wall so much that their garments would catch on the wall due to their clinging to it.)  Narrated by Abu Dawud (5272).
Sheikh Muhammad bin Ibrahim, the former Grand Mufti of the Saudi Lands, said:
> (And the point of evidence is: that if the Messenger of Allahpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyprevented mixing in the street because it leads to temptation, then how can it be said that mixing is permissible elsewhere?!)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (10/42).
> 
Ali bin Abi Talib, may Allah be pleased with him, said: (Do you not feel ashamed or have jealousy? For it has reached me that your women go out into the markets, crowding the 'Ulooj (1).)  Narrated by Ahmad (1118).
(1) Al-'Ulooj: Plural of 'Ilj, which refers to a disbelieving man of non-Arab descent.
Sheikh Al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And it was from the Sunnah of the Prophetpeace and blessings be upon him and his familyand the Sunnah of his successors to separate men and women... because the mixing of one group with the other is a cause for temptation; for when men mix with women, it is like mixing fire and wood.)  Al-Istiqamah (1/360).
> 
6. Women leaving their homes perfumed and scented: This action is not permissible; rather, it has come in the hadith that whoever does this is a fornicator. From Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari who said: The Messenger of Allah - peace and blessings be upon him - said: (Any woman who puts on perfume and passes by a people so they can smell her fragrance, is a fornicator.)  Narrated by An-Nasa'i (9361), and Ibn Khuzaymah (1681), and Al-Albani said: Its chain of narration is Hasan (good).
Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (Al-Haytami mentioned in 'Az-Zawajir' 2/37 that a woman leaving her home perfumed and adorned is among the major sins, even if her husband permitted her.)  Jilbab Al-Mar'ah Al-Muslimah (p.139).
>

Text:
7. Buying and selling firecrackers: Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (What I see is that buying and selling them is Haram (prohibited), and that is for two reasons. The first reason: it is a waste of money, and wasting money is prohibited due to the Prophet's, peace and blessings be upon him, forbidding of that. The second: it causes harm to people with its disturbing noises, and it may cause fires if it falls on something flammable while it is alive [lit] and not extinguished.)  Majmoo Al-Fatawa (3/3).
> 
8. Al-Qaza': Which is shaving part of the head and leaving part of it: From Ibn Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both: (That the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, forbade Al-Qaza'.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5921), and Muslim (2120).
And from him, may Allah be pleased with him, he said: (The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saw a boy who had part of his hair shaved and part of it left, so he forbade them from that and said: 'Shave it all, or leave it all.')  Narrated by Abu Dawud (4195).
It is said in Al-Qamoos:
> (Al-Qaza' is to shave the head of a boy and leave separate spots un-shaved, resembling the Qaza' (scattered patches) of clouds, and similarly in An-Nihayah.)  Ghidha' Al-Albab (1/429).
> 
Sheikh Abdul Mohsen Al-Abbad, may Allah preserve him, said in his explanation of Sunan Abi Dawud:
> (And included in Al-Qaza' is what some youth do in barbershops by shaving the hair at the nape of the neck seeking adornment; because shaving part of the head and leaving part of it leads to the same result, whether it is intended for adornment or not intended for adornment, except if the shaving is for medical treatment or for Hijamah (cupping), then there is no harm in this.)
> 
9. Girls wearing short clothing:
Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (I see that a person should not dress his daughter in this short clothing while she is young, because if she gets used to it, she will remain upon it and its matter will become trivial to her. As for if she is accustomed to modesty from her youth, she will remain in that state in her adulthood. And what I advise our Muslim sisters is to abandon the clothing of the people abroad from the enemies of the religion, and to accustom their daughters to covering clothing, and to modesty, for modesty is from faith.)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (12/274).
> 
10. Not listening to the Eid sermon: Some people, after praying the Eid prayer, leave and do not listen to the sermon, and this is a mistake. Imam Malik was asked about a man who prays with the Imam on the day of Al-Fitr, is it permissible for him to leave before hearing the sermon? He said: (He should not leave until the Imam leaves.)  Ahkam Al-'Idayn by Al-Faryabi (144).
 There are many violations that occur during Eid; we have mentioned some of them. I ask Allah to benefit me and you through them, and Allah knows best.

Text:
A Concise Summary on the Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid 

Sheikh Saleem Al-Hilli afidhahullh states:

All praise is due to Allh, Lord of the Worlds, and peace and blessings be upon the Master of the Messengers, Muammad, and upon his family, companions, and those who follow them with excellence until the Day of Judgment.

My dear brothers and sisters...There has been much discussion regarding this matter (i.e. Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid). Some have asked: Does one prayer suffice for the other, or is it obligatory for a Muslim to perform both the Eid and Jumuah prayers?

Many who have spoken on this issue have done so without the requisite qualification to understand the evidences transmitted from the Messenger of Allh . This is because they approach the matter based on a flawed methodology: they first adopt a belief, then seek out supporting evidence. This is a mistake! The correct methodology is to derive beliefs from evidence, not to impose ones beliefs or schools of thought upon the evidence.

Therefore, I shall briefly present the adths and reports attributed to the Messenger of Allh  and the noble Companionsmay Allh be pleased with themthen explain the positions of the recognised schools of Fiqh on this issue, relying upon Allh Almighty.

So I say, with Allhs guidance, that there are both prophetic traditions (marf) and reports from the companion (mawqf) on this matter.

The first is the adth of Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allh be pleased with him), in which Muwiyah ibn Ab Sufyn (may Allh be pleased with him) asked him: Did you witness the Messenger of Allh  having two Eids occur on the same day? He replied, Yes. He asked, What did he do? Zayd said, He performed the Eid prayer, then granted a concession regarding the Jumuah prayer, saying: Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray. This adth was narrated by Amad, Ab Dwd, An-Nas, Ibn Mjah, Ad-Drim, and Al-Hkim, and it is authentic.

The second adth is from Ab Hurayrah (may Allh be pleased with him), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we will indeed perform the Jumuah. This adth was also narrated by Ab Dwd and Ibn Mjah, and it is authentic.

The third adth is from Ibn Umar (may Allh be pleased with him), who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh . He led the people in prayer, then said: Whoever wishes to attend the Jumuah may do so, and whoever wishes to refrain may do so. This adth was narrated by Ibn Mjah. A-abarn also transmitted it with the wording: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh  on the day of Eid Al-Fir and Friday. The Messenger of Allh  led them in the Eid prayer, then turned to face them and said: O people, you have achieved good and reward. Indeed, we are holding the Jumuah. Whoever wishes to join us may do so, and whoever wishes to return to his family may do so. This narration is also authentic.

The fourth adth is from Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them both), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we are gathering, in-sh-Allh. This adth is also authentic and narrated by Ibn Mjah.

From the reports of the Companions: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the day. Then we proceeded to attend Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we mentioned this to him, he said: He acted in accordance with the Sunnah. This was reported by Ab Dwd, and by Ibn Khuzaymah with a different wording and with an addition: Ibn Az-Zubayr said: I saw Umar ibn Al-Khab, when two Eids coincided, do the same.

Text:
A tremendous evening with our brothers and sisters in London at @_MuSunnah in Alperton (Thursday 9th April 2026). 

Msh-Allh, the Markaz was filled with listeners. With barely a days notice, Ahlus-Sunnah travelled from all across London and beyond to join us. The evening began with a dars by Shaikh @uwaysT followed by Shaikh @AbuIdrees and finally a reminder from myself. 

It is important that we learn and convey this Deen with sincerity and good manners, becoming good examples for those around usthat we are humble and distant from the corrupting influences of social mediathat we take the Messenger Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) as our example, and then his Companions (Allah be pleased with them)that we love those those who love and follow them, for they are Ahlul-Hadth was-Sunnahand thereafter, the foremost among them in every age are the scholars (may Allah raise their rank). I can say that over the years, we have benefited greatly from the noble manners of the ulamshaping the way we behave and deal with the peopleand I seek pardon from Allah for my shortcomings.

So, in the West, our communities should focus on seeking knowledge; then act on what we learn, cultivate our children, nurture righteousness in our homes and hold fast to the Sunnah. 

Furthermore, Hijrah should be an important discussion in our households: actively planning and searching for a home in a Muslim country where there are already Salafi communities, however small. 

Know that Hijrah from non-Muslim lands to the lands of Islam is an obligationand a protection for you, your family and children; a safety net against the abandonment of religion today and in generations to come, insh-Allh. 

I also advise that you should not waste your time (and wealth) following the fashions, sinful habits and clothing of the non-Muslims because Islam has its own unique and proud heritage and identitynor should you follow and obsess over the modern-day offerings of entertainment (such as football, boxing and movies) that are designed to divert people away from the realities of life and its purpose: the worship of Allah and following the Messenger (peace and blessings be upon him). Focus, O Muslim, on what benefits you in this life and the Hereafter, brakallhu feekum. 

Do not be impressed by social media influencers who seek only views, likes and followers. Our example is the Prophet of Islamwho taught us to be humble, truthful and sincerethen his Companions and the Righteous Salaf. In these times you see that example reflected in the manners of the scholars and their conduct (may Allah preserve the living and have mercy upon the deceased). 

So, I advise you to get off your mobile devices and drastically limit viewing social media platformsinstead read books, attend duroos, get married, have children, focus on your families and build true Islamic homes. 

May Allah aid the Muslims and guide them to the Sunnah and the path of Ahlul-Hadthand that is the Madhhab of the Salaf. 

In thisby Allahthere is honour, just as Umar (Allah be pleased with him) said: We were a lowly people, and Allah honoured us with Islam. So if we seek honour in other than Islam, then Allah will make us lowly.

Abu Khadeejah.

Text:
Advice from Shaykh Abu Dujanah   (senior student of Imam Muqbil):

The Reprehensible Acts of Eid!
There are many violations during Eid that many people fall into. We will mention some of them so that we can be wary of them and avoid falling into them. These include:
1. Adorning oneself by shaving the beard: Know, my brother, that shaving the beard is forbidden (Haram) and a sin. The evidence for this is the hadith of Ibn Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (Trim the mustaches and let the beards grow.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5393).
And from Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (Trim the mustaches, and let the beards grow long; differ from the Magians.)  Narrated by Muslim (260).
Sheikh Ibn Baz, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And this wording in the mentioned hadiths necessitates the obligation of growing and lengthening the beards, and the prohibition of shaving and cutting them, because the default principle regarding commands is obligation, and the default principle regarding prohibitions is prohibition, unless there is evidence indicating otherwise, and this is what is relied upon by the people of knowledge.)  Majmoo Al-Fatawa (3/372).
> 
2. Dyeing the hair black: Know, my brother, that it is not permissible to dye the hair black, and a severe threat has been reported for those who do so. From Ibn Abbas, may Allah be pleased with them both, who said: The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, said: (There will be a people at the end of time who will dye their hair black like the crops of pigeons; they will not smell the fragrance of Paradise.)  Narrated by Ahmad (2470), and Abu Dawud (4212).
> (So this hadith is explicit regarding the prohibition of dyeing with black.)  Tuhfat Al-Ahwadhi (5/359).
> 
Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (And this hadith necessitates the prohibition of dyeing gray hair black, and that it is from the major sins.)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (11/123).
> 
3. Isbal (Lengthening the garment below the ankles): Isbal of the lower garment is one of the major sins because a severe threat is attached to it, and the one who practices Isbal has fallen into two prohibitions:
 * The First: He has exposed himself to punishment. From Abu Hurairah, may Allah be pleased with him, from the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, who said: (Whatever of the lower garment is below the ankles is in the Fire.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5787).
 * The Second: He has exposed his garment to dirt and filth. A young man wearing a long garment entered upon Umar bin Al-Khattab, may Allah be pleased with him, and he said to him: (O son of my brother, raise your garment, for it is cleaner for your garment, and more pious toward your Lord.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari in the story of the assassination of Umar (3700).
The Sunnah is for the lower garment and clothing of the Muslim to be at mid-calf. From Al-Alaa bin Abdul Rahman, from his father, that he said: I asked Abu Sa'eed Al-Khudri about the lower garment, and he said: I will inform you with knowledge; I heard the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, say: (The lower garment of the believer is to the middle of his calves. There is no sin on him for what is between that and the ankles. Whatever is lower than that is in the Fire, whatever is lower than that is in the Fire. Allah will not look on the Day of Resurrection at the one who drags his lower garment out of arrogance.)  Narrated by Malik in Al-Muwatta (2/914).
So the Muslim must beware of Isbal.

Text:
Ancient DNA Sample  Simple Summary
From #Akbari 2026

Published before Disparate demographic impacts of the Roman Colonization and the Migration Period in the Iberian Peninsula, deposited on bioRxiv under the DOI 10.1101/2024.09.23.614606.

This individual was a male buried around 623 AD in a cave burial site in northeastern #Spain (Girona, #Catalonia) during the Visigothic period.

Genetic Background

 Fathers lineage (Y-DNA): J1-L858
This lineage comes from the ancient Near East, especially regions like the Levant and Arabia, and is commonly linked to Semitic populations.

 Mothers lineage (mtDNA): H1
This is a typical Western European lineage, especially common in Iberia, showing local ancestry on the maternal side.

 Overall Ancestry

His genome shows a strong connection to eastern Mediterranean populations, especially groups historically related to:

Jewish communities of North Africa

Southern Europe (Italy, Greece)

His deeper ancestry is made up of:

Early farmers from Anatolia (Turkey region)

Significant Levantine/Natufian ancestry (ancient Near Eastern hunter-gatherers)

Smaller inputs from Iranian farmers, Caucasus groups, and European hunter-gatherers

What This Means

This man was not genetically Visigothic in the northern European sense.

Instead, he represents a mixed Mediterranean profile:

A Near Eastern paternal line

A local Iberian maternal line

A genome similar to eastern Mediterranean / North African Jewish populations

 Likely Historical Scenario

He was probably descended from people who moved west during the Roman Empire, such as:

Soldiers

Traders

Migrants from the eastern Mediterranean

These groups settled in Iberia and mixed with local populations. By the Visigothic period, their descendants were fully integrated culturally, even if their genetic origins were diverse.

 Key Insight

The term Visigothic here reflects culture and political identity, not a uniform genetic background.
This individual is a clear example of how migration and mixing in the Roman world shaped the population of early medieval Spain.

Text:
Ar-Rabah ibn Ab Abdir-Ramn said: 

Its not befitting for anyone who knows that he has something from knowledge to ruin himself. 

[Al-Madkhal (no. 687) of al-Bayhaq: a] 

Shaykh Muammad al-Uthaymn said: 

This is a good statement. How [does one ruin himself]? By neglecting the knowledge Allh gave him. Wasting knowledge occurs through neglecting it - not maintaining or caring about it. It also occurs by abandoning acting upon it i.e. that a person maintains it [through revision] but doesnt act upon it; this person is considered to have wasted knowledge, meaning that they didnt benefit from it. 

This should be an admonition for every student of knowledge to maintain what Allah granted them of knowledge. How? By reviewing, revising and acting upon it. For this reason, some [of the scholars] said, Preserve knowledge by acting upon it, while others said, Preserve it by writing it down, and both are correct [as one should do both]. 

(Shar a al-Bukhr)

Text:
Doubt: Shaykh Muqbil ibn Haadi, was a takfiri or made Takfir on Saudi

Responded by his student, Shaykh AbdulHameed az-Zukuri  :

Yahya Silmi As-Saylani (the Sri Lankan) says:
Sheikh Muqbils initial opinion, which caused a great uproar, was: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is a kafir country. He was firmly set on this statement, announcing it in Friday sermons. Then, when he went to Saudi Arabia before his death, he sat with its scholars, and they convinced him that it is a Salafi country, so he retracted and acknowledged that it is a pure Muslim country. [From the tape: Explanation of My Advice to Ahlus-Sunnah].

Sheikh Abdulhamid Az-Zukari responds:
So on Friday, the twenty-fifth of Rajab, in the year 1433 AH, we respond to this false statement, falsely and aggressively attributed to our sheikh, our father, and our teacher, Imam Muqbil ibn Hadi Al-Wadii, may Allah Almighty have mercy on himthe one whom Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, blessed and favoured by making him a reviver of this religion alongside other revivers, such as Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, Sheikh Ibn Baz, may Allah have mercy on him, and Sheikh Al-Uthaymin, may Allah have mercy on him. We have spoken about his way, his methodology, his biography, and some of what he adhered to in our book Al-Bayan Al-Hasan fi Tarjamat Al-Imam Al-Wadii wa Ma Ahyahu min As-Sunan. And we have responded to those who claim that Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, was a takfiri at any time or period in the book Awn Al-Bari li Bayan Hizbiyyati Ibnay Mari wa Man Jara Majrahum wa Radd Takharusat Abdullah Al-Bukhari. In that book, we explained the gist: that Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, was never a Haruri or a Khariji; rather, he was a Sunni Salafi ever since Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, blessed him with the knowledge of the creed of the Salaf, Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jamaah. Thus, he became a caller to it and a reviver of it. 

Among the evidence that shows the falsehood of Yahya Silmis statementthat Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, said about the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia that it is a kafir countryare the following matters:

First: Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, refuted Osama ibn Laden, refuted Abu Muhammad Al-Maqdisi, refuted Al-Masari, and spoke against Salman, Safar, and those similar to them who hold takfiri statements and deviant methodologies.

Second: We are certain and convinced that our sheikh, may Allah have mercy on him, never said this statement at any time because it contradicts reality, and Sheikh Muqbil was among the people most aware of the condition of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabiamay Allah grant it success and protect it with Tawhid.

Third: Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah have mercy on him, did not apply this description to countries that are less upright and less devoted to spreading good, let alone countries containing graves and shrines worshipped besides Allah, despite his belief that those who engage in grave-worship, those who call upon another god alongside Allah, those who slaughter for other than Allah, and those who make vows for other than Allah are disbelievers.

Fourth: Such an absolute statement is questionable. Even if we were to suppose that in that country there are acts of disbelief, or in Yemen there are acts of disbelief, or in Sudan there are acts of disbelief, or in Iraq there are acts of disbelief, this kind of absolute statementcalling any of the Muslim countries a kafir country outrightis questionable. This claim is contradicted by Sheikh Muqbils tapes, contradicted by the actual stance of Sheikh Muqbil, and contradicted by the students of Sheikh Muqbil.

Fifth: If Sheikh Muqbil had ever uttered this statement or something similar, the scholars would have condemned him for it.

Text:
Either Yahya Silmi received this statement from idle, malicious opponents of the call of Imam Muqbil ibn Hadi Al-Wadii, which has filled the plains and mountains, or he heard something similar to what Abdullah Al-Bukhari statedthat Sheikh Muqbil repented from the methodology of khuruj in the sense of his words two months before his deathand due to his poor command of Arabic and ignorance, he assumed they were accusing Sheikh Muqbil of saying that Saudi Arabia is a kafir country. It is sufficient to show a persons misguidance that he be a liar in what he transmits or inarticulate in his speech, for the inarticulate cannot express what is in his heart nor clarify what he intends, thus falling into something worse than what he wishes to deny. His situation is like what some of the Salaf said about Umar ibn Ubayd: Your shortcoming came from your poor Arabic, when he said, It is incumbent upon Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, to fulfill the threat. So I advise Yahya Silmi to fear Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, and I advise him to be humble and to seek knowledge.

The second matter: His statement, He sat with its scholars and they convinced himwhat did they convince him of? The fact is that it was conveyed to him, may Allah have mercy on him, that there was some injustice in Saudi Arabia and such things, so he would condemn that specific injustice that was reported to him; otherwise, he remained upon the Salafi creed. Evidence for this is that Sheikh Ibn Baz, Sheikh Ibn Uthaymin, Sheikh Rabee, Sheikh An-Najmi, and Sheikh Al-Albaninone of them, not in a single letter, accused Sheikh Muqbil of holding the opinion of the Khawarij or the takfiris, despite their knowledge of what he said or uttered, whether in what he wrote in Gharat Al-Ashirat or what he wrote in Al-Makhraj min Al-Fitnah or what he stated in other books. Then he retracted, may Allah have mercy on him, before his death; he retracted from all statements he had made about Saudi Arabia in that tape entitled My Observations in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia when he saw and heard the good in the country, as he explained the reason for his retraction.

The point of this introduction is: I declare this transmission and this statement from Yahya Silmi to be false. Sheikh Muqbil did not declare the Saudi government to be disbelievers nor did he say it is a kafir country. Scholars condemned Sayyid Qutb when he called it the Jahiliyyah of the twentieth century, and then Yahya Silmi comes and attributes such a statement to an imam of the Salafi call and a reviver of the Prophetic Sunnah, claiming that he used to say Saudi Arabia is a disbelieving country. So check your sources, O you who fabricates! 
{ } 
Cursed be the liars! 
Review the Arabic language and master it. If something is unclear to you, be humble and ask until you understand. But to become a fabricator who falsely attributes statementsand to whom?! To an imam known for the soundness of his creed and methodology! We did not accept from Abdullah Al-Bukhari nor from anyone else who claims that Sheikh Muqbil repented from khuruj. We say that Sheikh Muqbil never held the view of khuruj, nor do we accept that, neither at the beginning of his pursuit of knowledge nor at the end. Sheikh Muqbil was a Sunni Salafi from the time Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, enlightened him with the Book and the Sunnah. He denied this about himself, truthfully in what he said and conveyed, that he was never with Juhayman at any time, nor was he a supporter of him; rather, he condemned him, and so on. I refer to what I wrote in my book Awn Al-Bari fi Bayan Hizbiyyati Ibnay Mari wa Man Jara Majrahum wa Radd Takharusat Abdullah Al-Bukhari, and added to it is the refutation of the fabrications of Yahya Silmi Al-Saylani.

Source:

Text:
Fatawa will never suffice for replacing Ahkaam.

A Fatwa is not transferable or enforceable.

A Hukm applies to all until an exception is established.

Laypeople deal with Muftis and their Fatawa as sacrosanct because they don't have access to Fuqahaa' and Ahkaam.

Whenever you hear a person begin to speak about Islamic law on the basis of Fatawa, know they are a layperson who has no standing on the matter.

Whenever you hear Muftis begin to deal with Islamic law on the basis of Fatawa or the unique, independent Ijtihad of specific scholars, know that they are bereft of Ijtihad themselves.

Safety from the corruption and mistakes of scholars is found in sticking to the Mu'tamad of the four schools of Fiqh.

ONLY IF a Mu'tamad view for a matter is not found because of a lack of precedent, then scholars are turned to for guidance.

Engaging in Ijtihad/Ifta while well-established Ahkaam exist as legal precedent is irresponsible and usually involve a corrupt agenda.

Genetic Profile Summary   (Male)

1. Overall Ancestry Profile (Modern Context)

This individual fits squarely within the Western North African (Maghrebi) genetic spectrum, with strong ties to populations from:

Morocco (especially urban/northern regions like Fs)

Tunisia

North African Jewish communities (Libyan, Tunisian, Algerian)

Core Ancestral Components:

North African (Maghrebi / Berber-derived): ~3034%

Levantine (Eastern Mediterranean): ~2030%

Southern European (Mediterranean): ~1317%

Egyptian-related: up to ~18% (model-dependent)

Arabian Peninsula: ~36%

Sub-Saharan African: trace amounts

 Interpretation:

This profile reflects a classic Western Mediterranean fusion zone, where indigenous North African ancestry is layered with:

Eastern Mediterranean gene flow (Levant)

Southern European input (especially island/coastal populations)

Minor trans-Saharan and Arabian influences

2. Deep Ancestry (Prehistoric Breakdown)

Upper Paleolithic (~25,000+ years ago)

Ancient West Eurasian: 96.13%

Ancient African: 3.87%

Closest affinity:

Caucasus-related populations (ancestral to later West Eurasians)

Mesolithic (~10,00015,000 years ago)

A three-way structure typical of the Southern Mediterranean basin:

Natufian (Levant): 36.64%

Anatolian Hunter-Gatherer: 35.34%

Caucasus Hunter-Gatherer: 20.28%

Minor components:

Ancient East African (Mota-like): ~6%

Trace Ancient North Eurasian

Interpretation:

This combination represents the core genetic foundation of Mediterranean and Near Eastern populations, later transmitted into North Africa through migration and diffusion.

3. Paternal Line (Y-DNA): R-Y16006

Lineage Overview:

Belongs to the R1a-Z93 branch, associated with Bronze Age expansions

Emerged approximately 1350 BCE

Migration Context:

Originates from a lineage that moved: 

From Eurasian steppe regions

Into Central Asia  Near East  North Africa

Notable Features:

Rare in North Africa but present through historical migrations

Found today in:

Eastern Europe

Near East

Egypt

Known associations:

Some Jewish Levite lineages

Occasional links to elite or structured male line expansions

 Interpretation:

This paternal line likely entered North Africa through:

Levantine or Jewish-mediated migration routes

Or broader Bronze/Iron Age population movements

4. Maternal Line (mtDNA): M1b2

Lineage Overview:

Deep-rooted lineage dating to ~10,000 BCE

Part of haplogroup M1, which is unusual because: 

It originates outside Africa

Then returns via back-migration into North Africa

Historical Associations:

Strongly linked to: 

Early North African populations

Iberomaurusian cultural horizon

Mediterranean coastal populations

Modern Presence:

North Africa

Southern Europe (Spain, Portugal, Italy)

 Interpretation:

This is a native North African maternal lineage with deep prehistoric continuity and Mediterranean connectivity.

5. Integrated Genetic Interpretation

 represents a textbook Western Mediterranean North African profile, defined by:

Indigenous Maghrebi (Berber-derived) ancestry

Strong Levantine genetic influence

Noticeable Southern European admixture

Minor but real African and Arabian inputs

Lineage Contrast:

Paternal line: intrusive, likely from Bronze Age or later migrations

Maternal line: deeply rooted in North Africa since the early Holocene

 Big Picture:

This individual reflects the historical layering of the Maghreb, shaped by:

Prehistoric Mediterranean populations

Neolithic and Bronze Age expansions

Phoenician, Roman, and later Islamic-era gene flow

Jewish diaspora integration

Ibn Qudmah   said : 

Some of the righteous predecessors would dig a grave for themselves. Whenever they became fatigued from performing righteous actions, they would lower themselves into the grave, stretch out, and say to themselves:

'O my soul, imagine that you are dead and now in the grave. What would you wish for?'

The soul would respond, 'I would wish to return to the duny (worldly life) so that I may perform righteous actions.'

The person would reply, 'You have been granted what you wished for. So get up and do righteous actions (before it is too late).

[Wasiyah 11]

Text:
Ibn Rajab mentions in Ahwl al Qubr that some scholars mentioned that these 3 are singled out because:

The grave is the first stage of the Hereafter, offering a sample of the Day of Judgments consequences, not the full punishment/reward. 

Sins fall into two categories:
1.Rights of Allah (primary example: prayer)
2.Rights of servants (primary example: sanctity of life)

In the barzakh (the intermediate phase between death and Judgment), people are are not judged for the major sins themselves, but for their preludes and causes of lacking in them:

The prelude to prayer is to purify oneself of impurity and filth 

The prelude to violating the sanctity of life is slander and backbiting

So if a person failed to pray properly or violated the sanctity of a persons life, he will see the punishment for that on the Day of Judgment, but since these three (impurity, slander, backbiting) are lesser harms and foundational causes of these major sins, punishment in the grave begins with them; serving as an early consequence before the full reckoning on the Day of Judgment.

Text:
In a Al-Bukhr, from Ab Ubayd, the freed slave of Ibn Azhar, who said: I witnessed the two Eids with Uthmn ibn Affn on a Friday. He performed the prayer before delivering the sermon, then said: O people, today two Eids have coincided for you. Whoever among the residents of the outskirts (Al-awl) wishes to wait for the Jumu'ah, let him do so; whoever wishes to return home, I give him permission.

Based on these authentic prophetic traditions and reliable reports from the pious predecessors, the majority of scholars have concluded the following:

First: Whoever attends the Eid prayer is granted a concession from attending the Jumu'ah prayer. He may instead pray the Dhuhr prayer at its time. However, if he chooses to attend the Jumuah prayer with the congregation, that is more virtuous and better.

Second: Whoever did not attend the Eid prayer is not included in the concession. Therefore, the obligation of Jumuah remains upon him, and he must go to the masjid for the Jumuah prayer. If the minimum number required to establish Jumu'ah is not found, he performs Dhuhr instead.

Third: The Imm of the masjid is obliged to hold the Jumuah prayer that day, so that those who wish to attend it or those who did not attend the Eid prayer may do so. This is the explicit statement of the Prophet : Indeed, we are going to establish Jumuah prayer.

Fourth: Whoever attended the Eid prayer and avails himself of the concession not to attend Jumuah must pray Dhuhr. The Dhuhr prayer does not fall away under any circumstance, because what is lifted is the obligation of Jumuahnot of Dhuhr. Those upon whom Jumuah is not obligatorysuch as the sick, the traveller, women, and childrenare unanimously required by the scholars to perform Dhuhr.

Fifth: The opinion that whoever attends the Eid prayer is exempt from both the Jumuah and the Dhuhr prayers for that day is a weak opinion, though attributed to some Companions such as Ibn Az-Zubayr and Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them). This conclusion may be explained in several ways:

First, perhaps those who held this view were unaware of the adths that explicitly grant a concession from attending Jumuah, but affirm the obligation of Dhuhr for those who do not attend Jumuah.

Second, Ibn Az-Zubayr may have combined the Eid and Jumuah prayers at the time of the early Jumuah (before the sun reached its zenith). This is supported by a narration from Jbir ibn Abdullh (may Allh be pleased with him): We used to perform the prayer with the Messenger of Allh , then return and let our camels rest. This was reported by Muslim. Also, Sahl (may Allh be pleased with him) said: We would neither nap nor eat lunch until after Jumuah. This is the position of Imm Amad and Isq ibn Rhawayh (may Allh have mercy on them).

Additional evidence indicating that Ibn Az-Zubayr did this includes narrations such as: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the morning. We then went for Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we told him, he said: He followed the Sunnah. In another narration: A Friday and Eid coincided under Ibn Az-Zubayr, who said: Two Eids have come together in one day. So he combined them into one, prayed two rakahs early in the morning, and did not add anything until he prayed Ar.

This is a clear and explicit text that he included the Jumuah within the Eid prayer. And whoever performs the Jumuah does not then perform Dhuhr. Therefore, when this was reported to Ibn Abbs, he said: He acted upon the Sunnah.

This, dear brothers and sisters, is a concise yet beneficial explanation regarding the concurrence of Jumuah and Eid. I ask Allh, the Exalted, to have guided me to speak in accordance with the Sunnah and to have brought clarity on this issue to the general Muslim public...

Text:
Israeli Forces Tortured Child in Front of His Father in Gaza  

Israeli forces subjected a one-year-old child to abuse and torture to pressure his father into giving confessions in Al-Maghazi refugee camp in central Gaza, according to local and family sources.

Osama Abu Nassar, while taking his child to buy supplies a few days ago, he was caught in gunfire near his home and forced by Israeli soldiers to leave his 18-month-old son on the ground and approach the Israeli checkpoint, where he was stripped of his clothes.

According to witnesses, the army then took the child and interrogated the father at the checkpoint. The forces tortured the child in front of his father, including burning his leg with cigarettes, pricking, and inserting a metal nail into his leg, as confirmed by a medical report.

The child, Karim, was released after 10 hours and handed over to his family through the International Red Cross, while the father remains in detention.

 Osama Al-Kahlout

Text:
Sayn Cumhurbakanm bir konuda sizi uyarmak zorundaym. mrm sizin davanza hizmet etmekle geti ama Gazze konusunda yanl yapyorsunuz. 

Gazzede insanlar diri diri yanyor, alktan can veriyor, bir avu gda ve su iin birbirlerini eziyorlar. 
Siz ise hl BM'den, AB'den ve dier zulm birliklerinden medet umuyorsunuz. u bir hakikat ki Mslman'n kardei yine baka bir Mslmandr. Tarih boyunca Mslmanlara yine sadece Mslmanlar yardm etmitir. Zira kfr tek millettir. Yani sizin arlarnz beyhudedir ve kafirlere bel balamak Mslmanlarn sadece izzetini zedeler. Siz ve bu yazy okuyanlar unu dnebilir ya da syleyebilir: "u anda gcmz az ve ekonomimiz zayf." Maalesef bu dnce slm deildir. Bu dnce Allah' hesaba katmayan bir dncedir. Rabbimiz ayetinde " Neden Allah yolunda ve zayf dm erkek, kadn ve ocuklar iin savamyorsunuz? yle zayflar ki; Ey Rabbimiz! Bizi, ahalisi zalim olan bu ehirden kar; bize kendi katndan bir sahip kl ve bize kendi katndan bir yardmc ver. derler. (Nisa 75. Ayet) buyuruyor. Bu ayet vb. ayetler varken nasl oluyor da birtakm dnyevi kayglarla harekete gemiyorsunuz. Bu vaheti sizin knamalarnz ya da diplomatik giriimleriniz durduramaz. nk muhatap olduumuz varlklar insandan da aa bir topluluk ve bu aalklar yaptklar vaheti ibadet gryorlar.
slm, zulm karsnda ertelemeyi deil, kyam emreder!
Hadisi erifte Allah'n Resul:
Kim bir ktlk grrse, eliyle dzeltsin. Gc yetmezse diliyle, ona da gc yetmezse kalbiyle kar koysun. Bu, imann en zayf derecesidir. (Mslim, mn 78) buyuruyor. 
Biz hep imann en zayfna m talip olacaz. Biz, Bedir'deki 300 Mslmandan daha m azz ya da silahszz ki hep glenmeyi ve zaman hesaplar olduk. Kffara kar yaptnz hazrlk ALLAHn yardmndan daha m gl. Bu mmet ALLAHn yardm ile ne zalimleri devirdi. Hangi silah ALLAHn yardmndan daha gl olabilir. 

Bugn sessiz kalan, yarn zalimin orta olur!
Glenmeyi bekleyenler, zalimin mrn uzatr!
Halk icraat bekliyor.
Bu zulme susulan hergne Hem tarih hesap soracak hem de Rabbimiz.

Artk hamas aklama ya da knama deil, adm atn!
Unutmayn ki mazlumun feryad, liderin snavdr!
Ya imdi, ya asla Sayn Erdoan!
Bu millet sizi One Minute ile hatrlyor. Ama imdi sadece sz deil, fiil duru grmek istiyor!

Adalet ertelemeye gelmez. Zulm seyredilemez. G gelince deil, zulm varken susulmamaldr. 
Bu uyarm da u hadis mucibince yapyorum: "Sizden nceki mmetler, ilerinden biri gnah ilediinde, dieri onu uyarmazd. Sonunda hepsi helak oldu. (Eb Dvd, Melhim 17)" topluca helak olmamak adna yapmanz gereken islam devletleri bakanlarn toplayp sraili ve destekilerine kar topluca sava karar almanz. Sizle birlikte savamayacak devlet bakanlarn aklayn. Savaa girmeyecek korkak devlet bakanlarnn gerek yzn kendi halklar grsn. Siz Gazzeye yryn sadece Mslmanlar deil dnyann her kesinden farkl dinlere mensup milyonlar hatta yz milyonlar sizle birlikte Gazzeye girsin. SRALN Ate aarsa sava emri verin. 
Ya hep beraber lrz ya hep beraber diriliriz. 
slm  mmeti ve milletimiz adna seslendim. Selmetle.

Text:
Shaykh ammd Al-Anr (may Allh have mercy on him) said:

When I arrived at a place near Makkah, I met some Bedouins and asked their leader: What are the pillars of Islam?

He replied, Ash-Shfi, Ab anfah, Mlik  and I forgot the fourth one!

So I said to those around him, Remind him  mention the fourth.

They said, We have forgotten.

I then said to them, Amad (ibn anbal).

They all exclaimed together, Yes, thats the one!

He added:

This happened when we went out for dawah in the year 1375 AH.

[Collected in his biography]

Text:
Sincerity

Abu Bakr at-Tutl (d.270H) said:

Know that among the foremost things upon a servant is that he make all of his deeds purely for Allah. Much can be said on this topic, but its foundation, sincerity in ones actions, is this:

That you do a deed seeking only Allah by it, and you do not like that anyone among people should come to know of it. So if someone does happen to see your deed, you dislike that in your heart; you are not pleased by it, and you do not want anyone to praise you for anything you have done. Nor do you use it to gain status or standing with them. This is the foundation of sincerity in action, Allah willing and Allah is the One whose help is sought.

[at-Tutl, Kitb az-Zuhd]

Text:
The young lion , mujhid and martyr of Chechnya 

Muhammed Yutaev (Chechen:  ), also known as Magomed Yutaev, was a young Chechen fighter born in 1980 in Shalazhi, a village in Chechnya. He is remembered for his role in the First and Second Chechen Wars during his adolescence.

In early 1995, at the age of 14, Yutaev voluntarily joined the popular militia of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria to defend against the Russian invasion that began in December 1994. His commitment to the cause led to his selection for participation in significant operations, including Shamil Basayev's raid on Budyonnovsk in June 1995, a pivotal event in the war.

On May 21, 1996, Yutaev gave an interview to a Chechen presidential broadcaster, which brought him national recognition. Later that year, in August, he was involved in the Battle of Grozny, also known as Operation Jihad, which contributed to the partial liberation of the capital and was instrumental in securing victory in the First Chechen War.

At the end of 1999, during the onset of the Second Chechen War, Yutaev was captured by Russian forces. It is believed that he died due to torture suffered in Russian prisons . He is considered a martyr by Chechen separatists and was posthumously awarded the title "Qoman Turpal" (Hero of the Nation).

Yutaev's legacy endures as a symbol of youthful bravery in the Chechen resistance. His 1996 interview remains available online, serving as a testament to his dedication and the broader struggle for Chechen independence.
