Text:
To add one more thing: Iranian nationalists always ignore is a map they can never answer. The average Iranian is not even aware of this basic fact, and once you see it, the fairy tale of a uniquely ordained Iran with natural borders uniting Aryans since antiquity collapses. But as a starter, Iranian speaking peoples did not even originate on the Iranian plateau at all. They migrated into it. Before them, the Elamites were already there, non Iranian in language and identity, with a civilisation LONG predating the arrival of Iranians. The same applies to Kurds, who long predate the modern Iranian state and were later absorbed into a Persian nationalist project they never consented to.
The geology alone destroys the myth. The Arabian plate penetrates deep into what is now called Iran. Ahwaz, historically known as Arabistan even under the Safavids, sits on the Arabian plate, not the Iranian microcontinent. That is why its climate, landscape, and historical orientation align with Mesopotamia and Arabia, not the Persian heartlands. This is not ideology. It is geology. These are factsz simple as.
The same fantasy is imposed on Kurdish lands. The Zagros was never a sacred Persian boundary. It was a zone of movement and autonomy for Kurds and other highland peoples, later subdued through conquest and centralisation.

So when Iranian nationalists talk about natural borders, what they really mean is simple. These are territories they conquered early enough that they now pretend geography itself demanded it. This is not destiny. It is conquest rewritten as nature.,50.0

Text:
Truth Before Ties

Adh-Dhahabi, in his biography of Al-Husayn ibn Abi As-Surri Al-'Asqalani, said: Abu 'Arubah Al-Harrani said:
    . 
 He (Al-Husayn) is the maternal uncle of my mother, and he is a liar. [Mzn Al-Itidl (1/536)] 

Adh-Dhahabi, in his biography of Yay ibn Abi Unaysah Al-Jazari, said: Ubaydullh ibn Amr said: Zayd ibn Abi Unaysah told me: 
     .  
Do not write from my brother, for he is a liar. [Mzn Al-Itidl (4/364)] 

And he said in the biography of Al-Husayn ibn Abi As-Surri Al-Asqalani: Abu Dwd declared him weak. And his brother Muhammad said: 
     . 
Do not write from my brother, for he is a liar. [Mzn al-Itidl (1/536)] 

Adh-Dhahabi, may Allah have mercy on him, said in the biography of Abdullh ibn Jafar ibn Naj, the father of Al ibn Al-Madn: Ibn Al-Madn said: 
 . 
My father is weak. [Mzn al-Itidl (2/401)] 

Imam Abu Dwd Sulaymn ibn Al-Ashath said about his son Abdullh:
 . 
My son is a liar. [Mzn al-Itidl (2/433)] 

Adh-Dhahabi clarified: As for his fathers statement about him, it appears that if it is authentically attributed to him, he meant that he was a liar in his everyday speech, not regarding prophetic adth. It seems he said this when Abdullh was still a young man, but later he grew older and became a leading scholar. [Tadhkirat Al-uffdh (2/238)] 

Adh-Dhahabi said in the biography of Sad ibn Shubah ibn Al-ajjj:
 .
He is truthful.

Shubah said: 
      .
I named my son Sad, but he neither succeeded nor prospered. [Mzn Al-Itidl (2/122)] 

Adh-Dhahabi said about his son Ab Hurayrah:
       .
He memorised the Quran, then preoccupied himself with other matters until he forgot it. [Fat Al-Mughth (4/356)] 

Adh-Dhahabi said in the biography of Hishm ibn assn, Ab Abdillh Al-Qards:
   
He is trustworthy, a great and eminent Imam. Ibn Ad said: Amad ibn Muammad ibn Shabb narrated to us: Amad ibn Asad narrated to us: Shuayb ibn arb narrated to us: I heard Shubah say: 
           . 
If I were to show favoritism to anyone, it would be to Hishm ibn assnhe was my brother-in-lawbut he did not have strong memory. [Mzn Al-Itidl (4/495496)] 

Imam Muslim mentions in the introduction to his a:
ajjj ibn Ash-Shir narrated to me: Sulaymn ibn arb narrated to us: ammd ibn Zayd narrated to us: Ayyb said: 
                . 
I have a neighbour, and he is a man of virtue, but if he were to testify for me about two dates, I would not consider his testimony acceptable.

Al-fidh said in the biography of Al-Abbs ibn Mabb, known as Ibn Shnah: Maslamah ibn Qsim said:
       .
He is weak in adth; his narrations should not be written. He was a friend of mine. [Lisn al-Mzn (3/244)]

Text:
Upon the authority of Abu Hurairah, the Messenger of Allh (sallallhu 'alaihi wa sallam) said: 

When the month of Ramadan enters, the doors of Paradise are opened, the doors of Hellfire are closed, and the (chief) devils are chained.
[Bukhari, Muslim]

Shaykh Ibn Baz (May Allh have mercy on him) mentioned in explanation of the above hadith:

"This indicates the virtue of this great month and that Allah, Glorified and Exalted, grants His servants success in performing many righteous deeds during it, deeds for which the doors of Paradise are opened and the doors of the heavens are opened, as occurs in another narration: The doors of the heavens are opened.

In this is an encouragement to increase in acts of goodness such as prayer, giving charity, saying Subhnallh, saying L ilha illallh, and other than that to seize this opportunity; the opportunity of this great month and what it contains of Allahs generosity, His kindness, and the vastness of His benevolence.

Likewise, (seizing the opportunity found in) the chaining of the (chief) devils and their inability to reach what they used to reach (of evil) in other months, and that their evil is weakened."

 Explanation of Shaykh ibn Baz on Riyd As-Slihn (2nd explanation, no. 389) - taken from the Shaykh's website.
normalized_text: Upon the authority of Abu Hurairah, the Messenger of Allh (sallallhu 'alaihi wa sallam) said: When the month of Ramadan enters, the doors of Paradise are opened, the doors of Hellfire are closed, and the (chief) devils are chained. [Bukhari, Muslim] Shaykh Ibn Baz (May Allh have mercy on him) mentioned in explanation of the above hadith: "This indicates the virtue of this great month and that Allah, Glorified and Exalted, grants His servants success in performing many righteous deeds during it, deeds for which the doors of Paradise are opened and the doors of the heavens are opened, as occurs in another narration: The doors of the heavens are opened. In this is an encouragement to increase in acts of goodness such as prayer, giving charity, saying Subhnallh, saying L ilha illallh, and other than that to seize this opportunity; the opportunity of this great month and what it contains of Allahs generosity, His kindness, and the vastness of His benevolence. Likewise, (seizing the opportunity found in) the chaining of the (chief) devils and their inability to reach what they used to reach (of evil) in other months, and that their evil is weakened." Explanation of Shaykh ibn Baz on Riyd As-Slihn (2nd explanation, no. 389) - taken from the Shaykh's website.
